headers: Do you issue before going out for the funds?

data.Carrier Contact: Agency Customer Care Phone: 888-585-9198. Banner Life General Service: customerservice@bannerlife.com. William Penn General Service: customerservice@wpenn.com.
data.Required Paperwork: 1035 Exchange Form aka Absolute Assignment Form (LU1017)
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: No
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: Yes, 1035 exchange premium is requested from the carrier being replaced after the policy has been issued and the GA confirms that we are to proceed with the 1035 Exchange.  Policy will not be Delivered until funds are received.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: 6 months
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: We cannot accept a 1035 with an outstanding loan.

data.Required Paperwork: Replacement forms as required by the state. Section 1035- Beneficiary Designation/Ownership Change and Collateral Assignment Form CLI-6302.
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: No. However recommend maintaining until 1035 received by carrier.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: Paperwork has to be dated the same as the application. Typically after sixty days, if new application is needed, then a new 1035 form is also needed.
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: Not available.

data.Required Paperwork:
Correct state replacement form
    
	 Illustration
    
	 Original Absolute Assignment Form (for AGL: AGLC0010-2011 Rev0315 & for USL: 00850001-1021 Rev0316
    
	 Original Policy (if the original policy is no longer available, please check the Certificate of Lost Contract option on the Absolute Assignment Form).


A 1035 amendment may be needed to initiate the 1035 exchange process. It cannot be a delivery requirement. It is based upon the approval and if any additional items need to be amended.

Also, some carriers may request their own specific form in addition the forms listed above.
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: Only if the existing carrier requires it.
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: Only when instructed by the BGA.  The normal procedure is to retrieve the funds, then produce a policy.  If we issue before funds are received we require an updated illustration with 1035 funds removed.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: It depends on the existing carrier.  We will submit what we have.  If the existing carrier requests current dated paperwork, then we will notify the BGA.
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: We follow a 50/50 Loan Rule that states the amount we assume cannot be 51% or more of the amount of money the company received.  This information is determined on the the illustration.  Please know some carriers will require a loan to be paid in full prior to releasing any remaining funds to us.

data.Carrier Contact: Please contact your Replacement Analyst assigned to the case for any questions or contact the Customer Service Replacement Analyst team by email: replacements@jhancock.com or their dedicated phone line: 800-500-3101
data.Required Paperwork: The following criteria must be met before John Hancock will initiate a 1035 exchange: A final underwriting offer has been made once the final proposal/illustration has been reviewed; All pre-issue requirements have been received and are in good order; Confirmation that the client has accepted the final underwriting offer and their intent to proceed with the 1035 exchange. Note: We require an email confirmation from the firm that the client accepted the final offer and wants to proceed with the 1035 Exchange. The replaced carrier may not reinstate the coverage if the client changes his/her mind. This can result in a loss of coverage and significant tax consequences) Once these requirements have been completed, we can then initiate the request to begin the exchange. Please note that some carriers have their own forms that clients will need to complete in addition to John Hancock replacement forms. Some of these carriers are: MassMutual, RiverSource, Northwestern Mutual, Principal Financial Group, Knights of Columbus, Mutual Trust Financial Group, Nassau Life. This list of companies is subject to change without notice. Please note for Internal 1035 Exchanges: The email confirmation must be on or after the Underwriting decision date; same day effective date applies if the confirmation is stamped up until market closing at 4pm EST. Anything after this time gets next business day's date.
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: John Hancock recommends all External 1035 Exchange forms be wet signed; copy of wet signature can be submitted. Note: Please retain hard copy of original wet signature in case external carrier requires. Depending on amount of 1035 proceeds, other carriers might require an original wet signature and signature guarantee or notarized form.Note: If using the enhanced iPipeline iGO eAPP platform the Internal and External 1035 forms will be e-Signed along with the application package and submitted to New Business with the application up-front.  If an external carrier requires a wet-signature, the necessary requirements will be added as outstanding with a request for wet-signed forms.
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: We hold issue until all 1035 funds have been received.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: For 1035 Exchanges: most carriers recognize 90 days as a stale date period. However, this is subject to each carriers review, as Riversource recognizes a 60 day stale date period.
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: Yes, we do carryover existing loans. However, please confirm in advance with the Replacement Analyst if the 1035 Exchange carrier also participates.There is not a set ratio of total 1035 to loan that we accept. However, for further inquiries - please contact National Sales Support Team at: 888-266-7498, option 2.

data.Required Paperwork: Exchange Agreement (LF06591), unsigned illustration (signed if offer is rated), State Replacement form (if applicable), Amendment, DOI and any carrier-specific requirements
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: Currently Nassau Re (original surrender form with Medallion Signature Guarantee if proceeds exceed $100K), Security Mutual, and Riversource (original surrender form if 1035 proceeds exceed $100K).
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: Lincoln will request 1035 exchange funds upon approval of the application and issue the policy upon receipt of the funds.  We will consider rare exceptions to issue the policy prior to the receipt of funds on a case by case basis, and only in the customer's best interest.  Policy placement and commissions will generate once all funds and any delivery requirements are received in good order.  No exceptions are made in the state of NY.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: Generally 6 months for most carriers.  Some carriers require signatures to be dated within 30, 60, or 90 days
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: Generally, outstanding loan to cash value can be no more than 10%, but please defer to the illustration software on a case by case basis to determine the maximum loan amount based on all illustration variables.

data.Required Paperwork: In addition to the application we need the P4051 Assignment form to execute the 1035 exchange 1035 exchange – if a loan carryover is requested, we also need the F7010 for loan transfers.
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: Yes, most carriers require wet signature
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: Upon request, but NOT for loan carryovers
data.How long is paperwork valid?: App is good for 6 months but a 1035 can take up to 6 months but policy has already been issued so it’s n/a.
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: Net 1035 proceeds must be at minimum $10,000 not inclusive of the loan

data.Carrier Contact: There is not one specific contact, each case will be assigned to a 1035 associate for processing. This is a 4 person team dedicated to processing the 1035 exchanges. The person handling the exchange varies based on who does the Good Order Review for the policy.
data.Required Paperwork: 1. The NB application Part A Section 15 information needs to be completed (including the clients name, relinquishing company, policy number, To Be Replaced; checked Yes,1035 box checked Yes2. If the state requires, The state Notice of Replacement Form (In NY cases, REG 60 paperwork)3. The 1035 VLO Form4. The Illustration
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: This requirement varies between relinquishing companies. More often than not, E signatures are immediately rejected from the relinquishing company, and a wet signature is mandatory. Some companies diligently review and compare signatures on the 1035 forms to signatures they have on file, and will reject the exchange if they don't match. So making sure the signature is legible is important as well.
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: A 1035 exchange can only be added prior to a policy being placed fully In Force and active in our systems. It cannot be added post policy being placed In Force. We can issue a policy prior to the funds being received from the relinquishing company. To do so, the funds need to be illustrated as being applied, typically between month 2-3 in the first policy year. Best practice and normal practice is completing the exchange prior to issuing the policy. This is because if the amount that arrives is different at all we may have to do a tear-down / re-build of the contract which can lead to a chargeback & duplicate paperwork
data.How long is paperwork valid?: Each relinquishing Carrier has their own specific stale date for 1035 forms and replacement paperwork.  From a Nationwide perspective though, applications are good for 6 months
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: If the Loan Value to Cash Value Ratio is 90% or higher, we cannot accept it

data.Required Original Ink Signatures: Some companies require

data.Required Paperwork: 1035 Exchange Agreement and paperwork as determined by the delivering company. If 1035 is being initiated prior to policy being issued, the 1035 Exchange Confirmation form is also needed; this form is generated by the home office after approval. If the 1035 is being initiated after the policy is delivered, the Delivery Receipt will be needed for health confirmation.
data.Required Original Ink Signatures: No, if the delivering company does we will notify you asap
data.Do you issue before going out for the fu: Yes, this is an option. Delivery requirements would then be needed before we'd go out for the funds. In order to initiate the 1035 Exchange, the client must provide signed confirmation that his or her health has not changed since time of application and that he or she accepts our offer. Once an underwriting decision is made, this signature can be obtained one of two ways:Option A: Obtain the client's signature on a 1035 confirmation form Option B: Policy DeliveryOption A: 1035 Confirmation form at the time the case is approved and in good order, we will generate a client specific document that allows the client to confirm that there have been no health changes and that our offer is accepted. This form can be created and sent to the Case Contact within 24-48 hours after approval. The policy owner and insured will need to sign and date this document. We will initiate the 1035 exchange within 24 hours of receipt of this document in good order. The policy will not be issued until we receive the 1035 funds from the delivering company. Option B: Policy Delivery once the policy is approved and in good order, we will issue the policy. We will initiate the 1035 exchange within 24 hours of receipt of the delivery receipt in good order. If necessary, the policy can be reissued once the actual amount of 1035 funds is known.
data.How long is paperwork valid?: 12 months if < age 70, if the delivering company has other guidelines we will notify you asap.
data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: We do not accommodate outstanding loans

data.Max Loan/Cash Value Ratio: SML will consider a 1035 exchange with an outstanding loan subject to the following:  The outstanding loan on the policy to be exchanged cannot exceed 50 % of that policy's gross cash value. The loaned policy may be exchanged to a single life universal life policy. The minimum specified amount for the new policy is $250,000. For full details please contact Chelsea Mozley or April Hoover.

data.E-Policy / E-Delivery: How do I request eDelivery? To request eDelivery on an eApplication, simply answer the delivery question; I want my policy delivered electronically via email” within SimpleSubmit®. To request eDelivery on a paper application, submit the eConsent form number O-2000 with the application. What are the eligibility requirements for the eDelivery process? • Application submitted via SimpleSubmit or a paper application submitted with the eConsent form number O-2000• New Business cases where the insured, owner, and payor are the same person. (Policy Change cases are not eligible at this time) • Available on all products • Email address on file for the client and the soliciting agent

data.AMS Pending Case Downloads: Pending Case Downloads are available on the following platforms: AgencyWorks, SmartOffice and direct feeds in the ACORD format. Downloads are available Monday - Saturday, running every 2 hours between 8am to 8pm ET. Contact your Pacific Life New Business Supervisor if you would like to establish the pending download for any of the above.

data.E-Policy / E-Delivery:
eDelivery is available on an "All In" and "Case by Case" basis. There are also 3 options for first recipient: BGA, Producer, Policy Owner. The tool is available on a Pacific Life hosted site. When the policy is ready for delivery, the BGA will be notified by email to review and push the case to the Policy Owner, unless the Producer or Policy Owner as first recipient option is selected, in which case the email will be sent to them. 
Use of the eDelivery platform requires registration by the Producer on ProducerBay. The invitation to register for ProducerBay is sent in the welcome email from Producer Services.

headers: How can BGAs access statements? Paper, online?
Do you provide BGA commission feeds?  Yes / No
Do you provide Producer commission feeds?  Yes / No
Do you have any non commissionable policy fees
If you do, please provide details

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Statements can be accessed via the Banner Life Partner Dashboard.
data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: Yes. BGAs can request commission Excel feeds.
data.How can Producers access statements?: Statements can be accessed via the Banner Life Partner Dashboard.
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: BGAs can pull producer level statements by logging into the Banner Life Partner Dashboard, then clicking on Partner Information, then entering the agent code into the search field.
data.Are annualization of commissions allowed: Yes, only for Agents/Brokers holding a Level 10 contract.
data.If yes, please provide details: Agents/Brokers Level 10 are available to be set up on advances once the necessary paperwork is submitted and background check has been passed.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Commission payments can be received from Banner Life via ACH and frequency selection is made by the agent. Banner Life can also deliver commissions payments via FedEx Ground on a monthly basis.
data.Minimum commission distribution: Minimum payment for both check and EFT commission is $50.

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online through Agency Portal. Full downline view is available. No paper statements available.
data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: No. However .xlsx or .csv files are available by contacting cathy_stump@cinfin.com
data.How can Producers access statements?: Per the agency's request, producer accounts are established on Agency Portal upon completion of the producer's appointment.
data.Do you provide Producer commission feeds: No. However .xlsx or .csv files are available by contacting cathy_stump@cinfin.com
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Pay dates are 15th and last day of the month. Policy must be placed in force by 4 pm on commission run date to be included in that commission cycle.
data.Minimum commission distribution: $10 - accounts are emptied at end of year.

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Both
data.How can Producers access statements?: Paper and Online Agent Portal
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly and Yearly

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Agent portal, Connext
data.How can Producers access statements?: agent portal, Connext
data.Available in Excel?: Producers are able to pull Commission details in Excel format in Connext.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures:
· Commission cycle runs weekly beginning Friday after close of business through release day Tuesday.

· Commission is normally processed on Tuesdays (or the 2nd business day after cutoff).
o If Monday is a holiday (and not a business day), commission is processed on Wednesday.
o If the holiday is Tuesday, commission would be processed on Thursday, etc...
· EFT's deposits will occur on Wednesday
· Checks are mailed out on Wednesday
· If Wednesday is a holiday, then this process will occur Thursday

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online on JHSalesHub.com and emailed automatically
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online on JHSalesHub.com and emailed automatically
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: We do have option to enroll for eStatement but we don't have functionality on JHSalesHub.com at this time
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: We generate pay runs on every Friday and pay the commissions out the following Monday. DTCC feed is on Monday, Direct deposits are in accounts on Wed and Checks are mailed Tuesday
data.Minimum commission distribution: Direct deposit has no minimum. Checks have a $1,000 minimum
data.If you do, please provide details: Term policy fees are non commissionable

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Last business day of the week.  If a holiday falls on Friday, cutoff would be Thursday.

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: BGAs can receive their compensation statements as a CSV file via email, sent the Friday following each commission close. New BGAs are automatically enrolled once they provide a designated email address. Existing BGAs can be added upon request.
data.How can Producers access statements?: Producers can access their statements via the MMSD portal.
data.Available in Excel?: The CSV file is emailed to the BGA. Individual producers can download their statements in either CSV or PDF format.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Commission closes 1st and 3rd Tuesday of the month.. on the Friday after each close is when the direct deposits go in or checks mailed out

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Thursdays

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online through a Web access form. The process is handled through nfcomm@nationwide.com.
data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: Yes, Requests for feeds can be made to your Nationwide Business Relationship Manager and a clearing addendum will need to be signed
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online. Access can be setup by the producer without a web access form if paid directly. nfcomm@nationwide.com can assist with the step by step instructions.
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: No. Unfortunately, access is done at the Home Office level. What is paid to the downline producer however will be included on the BGA statement.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Nationwide is on a weekly commission cycle with the cycle wrapping up each Friday and comp being paid the following Monday.  If a holiday were to land on a Friday, the cycle would wrap up Thursday and if a holiday were to be on a Monday, payment would go out on Tuesday.
data.Minimum commission distribution: No minimum. If comp is due, comp is paid regardless the dollar amount.

data.Minimum commission distribution: $50 minimum for EFT funds to be sent

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online access is not available.  To request copies of statements email AMN_Disbursements@newyorklife.com
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online access is not available.  To request copies of statements email AMN_Disbursements@newyorklife.com
data.Available in Excel?: Available by request only.  Email AMN_Disbursements@newyorklife.com
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: Online access is not available.   To request copies of statements email AMN_Disbursements@newyorklife.com
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Compensation pay dates are the 15th and last business day of the month.  Premiums must be applied 2 business days prior to be included in the 15th and month end cycle. Off-cycle payments are subject to approval.  Contact your Regional Field Director for details.
data.Minimum commission distribution: No minimum for EFT payments; $3 minimum for check payments

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: By registering on OneSource Online and accessing the "My Business" tab, then "Commissions" in the drop down menu and either "One Statement" for monthly statements (available 3-5 business after the end of the month) or "One Daily Compensation Activity" for daily commissions activity.  BGA will need to have an active State Life Agency Code (ex. SCFR3)
data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: Yes, through DTCC.  Commissions would get paid through DTCC COM
data.How can Producers access statements?: By registering on OneSource Online and accessing the "Original OneSource Online" (see upper left corner once logged in).  Then click on the "My Business" tab, then "Commissions" in the drop down menu and either "One Statement" for monthly statements (available 3-5 business days after the end of the month) or "One Daily Compensation Activity" for daily commissions activity.  Producer will need to have an active State Life Agency Code (ex. SCFR3).  If Producer has assigned their commissions to another entity, only the entity can see commissions information by registering with OneSource Online and following the above steps.  The assignment of commissions entity will need to retrieve what their State Life Agency Code is from the OneAmerica licensing team at Licensing.CorpFin@OneAmerica.com in order to register for OSO.
data.Available in Excel?: Statements can be downloaded in Excel
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: Yes and No.  If producer allow delegate access to their OSO account they can give permission to access their commissions information.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Commissions are paid as earned, If you are looking for the commissions to hit by the last business day of the month, the case would need to issue at least 3 business days before month end.

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Online at Producer Bay - https://producerbay.pacificlife.com/
data.How can Producers access statements?: Online at Producer Bay - https://producerbay.pacificlife.com/
data.Available in Excel?: Commission statements can be downloaded from the Producer Bay website into a .csv file
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Commission payments are processed on Mondays unless it is a banking holiday which will then process on Tuesday. an email notification will be sent to the payee with a link to the Producer Bay website to access their statement.
data.Minimum commission distribution: Standard commission payment method is EFT with a minimum payment is $5. Paper checks on an exception basis only and have a minimum payment of $2500

data.How can BGAs access statements? Paper, o: Statements can be accessed online. We no longer mail paper statements (and also avoid emailing them)
data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: Currently, we do not send any management comp via feeds (DTCC).
data.How can Producers access statements?: These are accessed online.
data.Do you provide Producer commission feeds: Yes, we offer daily feeds via DTCC
We offer daily feeds via DTCC
data.Available in Excel?: We do not offer any producer statements in an Excel format (PDF only).  We do offer a CSV file for management comp but only under certain circumstances.
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: Only if the Advisor has contacted Principal and given the office delegate access to their statements online.  Because we offer dual contracting, advisor statements can have business through several different offices on one statement. This is why we don't grant BGA offices access to advisor commission statements.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Once premium is applied commissions generate overnight and then paid out on the next statement.
data.Minimum commission distribution: We have no minimum if the Advisor is set up on EFT.  We do have a $500 threshold before we will cut a check.

data.Do you provide BGA commission feeds? Ye: DTCC COM feeds are available with a completed DTCC Connectivity Request form.
data.How can Producers access statements?: Producers receiving comp can access their own statements on our website. Producers that assign their comp do not have access to statements. Special authorization from the assignee would be required. Contact lpmcomp@securian.com if further information is needed.
data.Available in Excel?: No - although commission data reports from our website (broker portal) can be saved in excel format.
data.Can BGA pull producer-level statements?: BGAs can access producer-level statements if the producer's comp is assigned to the BGA. Otherwise, BGAs only have access to their over-rides.
data.Cutoff dates / procedures: Once initial premium and delivery receipt is received, commissions will be released on the 3rd business day of the following week.  We prefer these items received by noon on Friday.
data.Minimum commission distribution: $25.00.If the commission is not at least $25, it will be released once it reaches $25. Otherwise, amounts less than $25 will be released at the end of the tax year.

data.Cutoff dates / procedures:
Commission Payments are made based on payment cycle cutoff dates as follows: 
Electronic Funds Transfers (EFT) will be posted to your account two business days following the payment cycle cutoff date, except holidays when they will be posted two business days following the holiday. Paper checks are mailed three business days following the cycle cutoff date, except holidays when they will be mailed the next business day following the holiday. If Symetra and NYSE are closed, no cycle will run on that day. That cycle will run on the next business day.
data.Minimum commission distribution: No minimum commission when paid via IPS or EFT.  For paper checks, there is a $500 minimum threshold before release

data.Cutoff dates / procedures: We have two pay cycles – Weekly and Daily. For Daily Pay cycle policy must be placed by 2:30 pm Central to be paid next business day.  Weekly pay policy must be placed by 5:00 pm Central Tuesday and is paid that following Friday.  Pay cycle is indicated on agents contracting and can be changed at any time.
data.Minimum commission distribution: Paper Checks are mailed once a year. Checks are sent at the end of each year.

data.Contact: General Inquiries: 800-839-5960 or ais@bannerlife.com. Case Management: 855-914-9115 or lga-libra@lgamerica.com. Escalated Inquiries: Amy Carpenter acarpenter@lgamerica.com or Lynn Clayton lclayton@lgamerica.com.

data.Other carrier exam accepted: Other carrier exams can be accepted assuming they are within the permissible time limits and provide build, BP and pulse.
data.Tele-interview process: 5 attempts on a left message status and 7 attempts on no answer/busy signal before the case is systemically closed.
data.Owner other than insured: Yes. The owner signature will also be collected.
data.Bind coverage: Yes. TIAA is offered if selected on the drop ticket.
data.How to obtain medical info on case: Underwriting decision and case package, including medical records, is sent to general agency via secure email. Information also available on Partner Dashboard.
data.Producer Contracting Verification: Yes, broker information is in the Partner Dashboard and displays appointment information.

data.Name: iGo drop ticket

data.Name: AG Quick Ticket
data.Contact:
ExamOne - 800-225-6344 or Dmdirectresponse@examone.com
For case status and general questions, please call Customer Service (877-686-7945) or email your dedicated pending requirements team (libra@corebridgefinancial.com).
data.Who orders APS: Corebridge Financial will order unless they are notified that the agency wants to order their own APSs.
data.Client email required: Email needed if application is being emailed or if client elects for e-signature
data.Other carrier exam accepted: No - it would not qualify for the Quick Ticket process
data.Tele-interview process: Voicemail messages will be left by the interviewer if the client is not reachable. Client may call back at their convenience (1-888-876-3407 for English; 1-866-768-2705 for Spanish). Client has 14 days to complete interview before case is closed.
data.Owner other than insured: Cannot use process if the payor is not the insured
data.Bind coverage: Yes, with a completed LTLIA form AND bank draft authorization form or credit card authorization form. (Credit card is approved for Term policies only, but not accepted in AK, MD, NC, NJ or NY. Payment must be submitted through Visa, MasterCard or American Express. Bank issued debit cards are acceptable; prepaid credit/debit cards are not accepted.)
data.How to obtain medical info on case: Forms required to retrieve records when a case is delined, approved at sub-standard, or submitted via Quick Ticket.
data.Producer Contracting Verification: The Quick Ticket link does not verify contractng and state appointment info against Connext.  The agency would need to look at Agent Lookup before using QT
data.Who cannot use the process: - Payor is not the insured  - Application is a trial / informal  - Requesting coverage for Spouse / Other Insured  - Replacements in New York - Telephone interview must be conducted in English or Spanish. Interpreters may not be used. - Do NOT use Quick Ticket if you plan to schedule the exam yourself or use another carrier's exam. In those cases, please submit a paper application.

data.Name: iGO - Permanent/Term Ticket (requires tele-interview)
data.Limitations: Available on Single Life Term and Perm products.Replacements are permitted.Not available in Guam and New York
data.Client email required: yes, it is needed for the eSign of the application and other necessary forms.
data.Other carrier exam accepted:
Other company exams can be accepted.

Please work with your John Hancock case manager to cancel the exam order.
data.Tele-interview process: When IFC Calls the Client:IFC will begin their initial outreach within 4 hours from receiving the ticketCalls continue every 24 hours for days 2 7Days 8 14 are met with every other day cadenceNote After the final attempts, the ticket will remain in the system, to allow for re opening if the Client calls back in.How to View Ticket Status:Ticket status (Most current status, coupled with any notes left by our staff)You will need to enter a ticket number to view statusThe ticket number will be a combination of the applicant year of birth, and the last 4 digits of their SSN.Please visit, IFC website: www.insurancefullfillmentcenter.com For status inquiries, please email: johnhancock@insurancefulfillmentcenter.com.Interview Phone Number: 833-399-6454 (Business Hours: Weekdays 8am to 8pm Central time)
data.Owner other than insured: Owner can be person, business or trust.  When the owner is the trust, additional paperwork will be required.
data.Application process: DocuSign Signature:  After the interview, IFC will send an email to the client.  Client logs in using last 4 of their SSN.  Reviews and applies their signature.  Agent Signs via Agent Attestation.  If owner is other than insured, the owner will get an email, using the last 4 of their SSN to log in (TAX ID for business or Trust), and apply their signature.After all signatures are gathered the application packet will be submitted to John Hancock to begin the New Business and Underwriting review.
data.e-Delivery:
eDelivery is not tied to the submission type. 

Please refer to eDelivery section for details.
data.How to obtain medical info on case: Can be downloaded from APPSLive.com if ordered with the interview.  For ExpressTrack cases, the Exam is ordered with a different Case Number.  If the case is not linked to the agency, they can ask for a copy from the Case Manager
data.Producer Contracting Verification: While agents do not need to be pre-appointed prior to Ticket submission, we do recommend getting appointed in PA, MT, WI, GU, PR prior to submission due to their state guidelines.

data.Name: VIVE
data.Limitations: Vive can be used for all single-life, term products that meet the following parameters:     	Insured ages 18 -79	    Face amount between $250K and $65M	    U.S. Resident	    The state of solicitation is any state other than Guam or New York 	    Ownership types: Individual, third party, corporation/business 	 Vive is not an option if:    there are multiple owners 	    the owner is a trust, trust to be established, partnership or qualified plan	    Aspire or ADB are elected
data.Who orders APS: John Hancock will order EHR if available. If no EHR within 5 days, John Hancock will order the APS
data.Client email required: Yes, the email is needed to send the Digital Part II to the client
data.Other carrier exam accepted: Other company exams can be accepted. Please work with your John Hancock case manager to cancel the exam order.
data.Tele-interview process: Not Applicable
data.Owner other than insured: Ownership types: Individual, third party, corporation/business
data.Application process: The producer begins the process by logging into Vive and answering the knock-out questions.      	If the submission is eligible for the digital Part II, the producer will complete some basic application information 	    The proposed insured will receive an email to log in, complete the lifestyle and medical questions and then e-Sign the application	    Upon application submission, the underwriting path will be emailed to the producer.  The proposed insured will either be considered for ExpressTrack or will go through traditional underwriting. Please note: reminder emails are sent on day 4 and 10 if the client has not accessed their application.

data.How to obtain medical info on case: Work with case manager
data.Producer Contracting Verification: While agents do not need to be pre-appointed prior to Ticket submission, we do recommend getting appointed in PA, MT, WI, PR prior to submission due to their state guidelines.

data.Name: LincXpress
data.Contact: Gina Hillson, Ops Manager (gina.hillson@lfg.com or 860-466-2785); Kelli VanDeusen, Ops Manager (kelli.vandeusen@lfg.com or 860-466-3197) Tricia Estrada, Team Lead (patricia.estrada@lfg.com or 800-225-4564 ext 61115), Katie Medina, Team Lead (katie.medina@lfg.com or 800-225-4564 ext 61170)
data.Limitations:
LincXpress paper and electronic ticket (eTicket) submission is available for:
o Lincoln LifeElements Level Term
o Permanent Life Insurance Products: o Indexed Universal Life (IUL) / Survivorship Indexed Universal Life (SIUL)
o Variable Universal Life (VUL) / Survivorship Variable Universal Life (SVUL)
LincXpress is available for all products, all ages and all face amounts in all states, except New York. Subject to product maximum ages and face amounts.
Exclusions: Lincoln LifeElements One-Year Term, Lincoln TermAccel©, and Lincoln MoneyGuard©. Internal Exchanges/Replacements, Term Conversions and Reinstatements cannot be processed using the LincXpress ticket submission process.
data.Fulfillment Vendor: Dedicated Lincoln Tele-App Team or online eInterview
data.Who orders APS: Lincoln no longer requires Age/Amount APS's.  The Underwriter will assess and determine if an APS is needed and will attempt to order an EHR, Claims data, or Personal History Interview (PHI) if available/applicable prior to ordering a traditional APS.  If a traditional APS is needed the BGA is responsible for ordering.
data.Client email required: Yes, client email is needed for e-signatures
data.Other carrier exam accepted:
For a LincXpress case submission, the Underwriter will have the ability to pause the automation process, so a decision can be made whether to use prior labs.
If prior labs will be used, the automated system will not auto-order labs and the case will require a manual Underwriting review of the labs for final approval.
[Note: Outside labs cannot be submitted or used for Lincoln TermAccel cases.]

To deliver a streamlined experience, Lincoln will order the labs and vitals after the client interview is completed, unless acceptable results are already in possession. The agent/agency should not order labs and vitals on a TermAccel or LincXpress case. 
If lab work was previously completed within the last 12 months for ages up to 69 and in the last 6 months for ages 70+, you may attach the lab slip, exam and/or medical records with the ticket.
data.Tele-interview process:
Online (eInterview) will be the default interview method; however, the agent can select the phone (Tele-interview) method within the Client Interview Type section in the electronic ticket submission process.

eInterview: • After the eTicket is received by Lincoln, a secure link will automatically be emailed to the client to begin their online interview. (Reminder emails will be sent on the 3rd, 5th and 8th day after the initial email is sent)
• The client must authenticate themselves by providing the last 4-digits of their SSN and consent to the electronic transmission process.
• The online interview experience is a fully electronic experience for the client and is estimated to take approximately 20-30 minutes to complete (if they have been properly prepared).
• eSignatures will be captured from the client.
Phone Interview: When case is deemed 'in good order', an email will be sent to your client with a link to Lincoln's Online Scheduling Tool to schedule their Tele-App interview at a time that's convenient for them. If no appointment is scheduled within 24-48 hours, a Lincoln in-house Tele-App Specialist will call the client to schedule. The client can also reschedule or cancel their Tele-App appointment using the online scheduling tool.
A phone interview, typically lasting 30-40 minutes, will be conducted by a dedicated Lincoln team member. An appointment reminder is available for the client upon request, via text message or email.  Voice signatures can be collected for TermAccel through LincXpress or for TermAccel and LifeElements through Vive.
data.Bind coverage:
Temporary insurance is available with TermAccel and LincXpress. Effective 11/15/21 a new TIA form is to be used that does not require advance payment to put the TIA into effect.  The client must instead agree to a stated amount (equal to the first modal premium) to be deducted from the death benefit upon death of the Proposed Insured per the terms and conditions as stated on the form.

Advance payment will continue to be required for Permanent products and Term full applications using Form LFF11524 (state variations)
data.How to obtain medical info on case: Lincoln will share labs and exam with the agency in cases where the client is declined or upon request if approved at a rate other than applied.
data.Producer Contracting Verification: When the agent is completing the ticket and enters his/her SSN or the agent flex code, a look up is completed in real time to determine if the agent's appointment is IGO.  If the appointment is not IGO, the agent must provide contact information and the contracting / licensing team will reach out to them within 24-48 hours to request a compbuilder, etc. in order to process the appointment.  The ticekt / quote can still be submitted to Linciln even if the agent's appointment is not IGO.
data.Who cannot use the process: State of NY Internal Exchanges Replacements Term conversions Reinstatements

data.Name: PLExpress App
data.Contact: If you are interested in onboarding for a ticket platform other than VIVE, please contact the PL New Business Supervisor.
data.Limitations: Ticket process is not available if the case is a Replacement for the states of Arkansas, Rhode Island or Wyoming.
data.Entry Options: Online ticket submission through the Vendor portals
data.Client email required: Client email is required for the case to be considered for Accelerated Underwriting "SwiftSailing"
data.Tele-interview process: Applicants are eligible for our online health history tool (About Me) if they meet age and amount guidelines: 18-70 and up to 2M in coverage.  Applicants also have the option to complete traditional phone interviews with Exam One.
data.Bind coverage: Temporary Insurance may be obtained if the TIA form is completed, total coverage is $1MM or less, and an in good order application packet and valid pay authorization are received.
data.Application process: On all drop ticket/express app cases - The Pacific Life case manager will reach out to the applicant directly to obtain outstanding underwriting requirements (unless they are specifically deemed to be the act of an agent).  The Pacific Life case manager will make outbound calls to the applicant every 5 days.
data.e-Delivery: Available as long as the BGA has completed the appropriate paperwork to have the feature turned on (BGAs can reach out to Pacific Life New Business Supervisor for help with this process).
data.How to obtain medical info on case: APS(s) will be available for viewing and PDF download on Producer Bay unless the records feature sensitive content.  Labs will not be shared on Producer Bay but can be requested by reaching out to the dedicated New Business team.
data.Producer Contracting Verification: Handled by our dedicated Licensing and Commissions team (Producer Services)
data.Who cannot use the process: Any BGA that requests the use of drop ticket submissions/the fulfillment process during their initial onboarding, or with a subsequent request to the New Business Supervisor, should be eligible to utilize this service.

The client can complete Part A, Part B online or on the phone. Once completed, this is sent to the applicant and the advisor for e-signature

data.Application process: See "Speed up your Term business with Drop Ticket

data.Name: 1. Fast App 2. Express Complete 3. Vive
data.Contact: Contact your dedicated New Business Case Manager

data.Name: Applications can be initiated electronically by a Drop ticket through iPipeline IGO©, ApplicInt or IXN.    	Applicint - To register for Applicint's ZipApp electronic drop ticket system, please call (775) 525-5220 or email support@applicint-use.com.    	iPipeline - To register for iPipeline's iGo electronic drop ticket system, please call (800) 641-6557, option 1 or email support@ipipeline.com.    	IXN - To register for IXN's electronic drop ticket system, please call (800) 250-5152, option 1 or email support@ixn.tech.com.
data.Contact: Brokerage Sales Team (888) 224-7254, option 1  Email: brokerage@sbli.com
data.Limitations: Products available: Term 10-, 15-, 20-, 25-, and 30-year durations Whole Life offers continuous-pay, limited pay (10y, 20y, and paid-up at age 65) And Single Pay premium options
data.Fulfillment Vendor: APPS is the exclusive fulfillment vendor for SBLI. APPS will complete Telemed Interviews and complete Part 1 and Part 2 of the Application and order the paramed (vitals and labs).
data.Who orders APS: Based on BGA preference; either BGA or SBLI
data.Other carrier exam accepted: Subject to approval by SBLI Underwriting, another carrier's exam can be used to underwrite a new application.  However, a non-medical Part 2 of the application will be required since we cannot issue the policy with another carrier's Part 2.
data.Tele-interview process: The proposed insured/client will be contacted one to two business days after submitting an application by APPS.  If the client cannot be reached on the initial call, APPS will leave a message with a call back number and urge the client to call APPS.  APPS will attempt several more follow up calls in an effort to reach the client.
data.Owner other than insured: After submitting a drop ticket, APPS will reach out to the proposed insured/client to completed the Part 2 of the application.  The owner's signature will be obtained by an Application Amendment or at time of delivery.
data.Bind coverage: Conditional coverage is available on each policy application through the Conditional Receipt Agreement (fully executed signatures on application, completed Part 2/Telemed Interview and/or Paramed, initial premium payment information recorded on Automatic Payment Plan Authorization form, Credit Card Payment form, or payment in form of two months of premium by check.
data.Application process: The agent drops a ticket via Applicint, iPipeline, or IXN.  APPS (a) conducts the Telemed Interview to complete the Application Part 2, (b) obtains voice or electronic signatures for applications Part 1 and Part 2, (c) if applicable schedules the paramed exam, and (d) electronically submits the completed application docs to SBLI.
data.e-Delivery: E-delivery is available if both the BGA and the insured opt-in at the time of submitting the new application. After the policy application is approved and issued, an email will be sent to the client providing a link to www.mysbli.com with instructions to a) register their new policy, b) review their policy, c) pay the initial premium via either credit card or bank EFT, and d) e-sign to confirm receipt of the policy package.
data.How to obtain medical info on case: Agents can obtain status updates underwriting information on the Policy Details page through www.SBLIAgent.com.  Specific requests for medical information can be submitted through the Case Coordinator link stated Policy Details page through www.SBLIAgent.com.
data.Producer Contracting Verification: A BGA can view their contracted downline on www.SBLIAgent.com.

If someone other than the client is requesting the information, we will need an Authorization of Release form completed.

data.Guidelines: It is recommended that Errors and Omissions liability insurance be maintained by every agent. All Banner agencies that hold the BGA and BMGA1 agreements are required to maintain Errors and Omissions insurance. Errors and Omissions insurance is not needed for corporations in the state of OK. Agent/Brokers are not required to maintain Errors and Omissions unless they are on advance commissions or licensed in Kentucky and Oklahoma. For William Penn, all General Agents are required to maintain Errors and Omissions coverage.
Required for all producer and agency appointments.
No

The person or entity appointed is responsible for maintaining at least $1 million per act of E & O coverage without interruption while the contract and appointment is active with American General and agrees to provide a copy of the E&O policy, if requested.  The person or entity appointed also acknowledges that this is a minimum level only, and if their E&O coverage needs are in excess of $1 million, they agree to ensure that their E&O coverage needs are addressed appropriately.
We do not require agent's E & O. GA is responsible for collecting that data.
A minimum of $1 million E&O coverage is required unless the producer is covered under their Broker Dealer Selling Agreement

You must acquire and maintain a Life Agents Errors & Omissions Policy with a  minimum coverage of $1,000,000 per claim/$1,000,000 per aggregate with a maximum deductible  of $50,000. Proof of insurance coverage shall be provided to the Company upon request. Does your company require E&O coverage? If so, is the agent or SMS required to submit updated E&O certificates?  Yes, we require both to hold E&O coverage. The agent or SMS only needs to provide updated E&O certificates to MassMutual upon request.  Do you accept E&O blanket coverage? Some agencies and individuals have a policy that covers all agents/employees in their hierarchy.  If the Agency has an E&O policy which covers all or some agents and employees, we would need to a copy of the declaration pages and exhibits which shows who is covered under that policy. Naming the downline relationships that are covered by the blanket policy.  Do you accept E&O coverage naming an agency on the declaration page?Yes but the policy must name the agent/corp that is being onboarded to ensure they are covered under E&O policy. Additionally, limits and retention amounts must be within our allowed threshold.If an agent is contracting their agency, is the agent name and agency name required to be listed on the E&O certificate? Yes both need to be listed
Proof of E&O is required

E&O is to be maintained by the agents themselves per our agent contract, and is required. Minimum requirements are $1 million per policy and $1 million aggregate	                            North American does not require the agent to provide proof of E&O (Declarations page), rather they need to provide their E&O Carrier Name and E&O Policy Number on the Agent Contract Application when initially on-boarded. Once an agent is contracted, their E&O is locked in for the duration of their contract with North American and we will not ask for renewal copies, as the agent is to maintain on their own.
OneAmerica doesn't require E&O
E&O coverage is required for contracted producers in an amount not less than $1MM
Minimum required E&O coverage of $1 million
$1,000,000 per claim minimum for every agent

The E&O Section of the CDS should only be completed when an individual or Firm is contracting directly with Prudential.   Prudential requires brokers to maintain E&O coverage with unimpaired limits of not less than $1 million.  Producers are required to evidence such converage by providing the Policy Number, Carrier and Coverage Amount on the Condifential Data Sheet appointment form.  Although we do not require a Policy Declaration or Coverage Page to be submitted with the CDS, we do reserve the right to request evidence of such coverage at any time.
SBLI requires $1 million of Errors & Omissions (E&O) coverage. A copy of the agent's Declarations page is required.
$1M minimum coverage required.   E&O confirmation can be provided on the Broker Data Sheet or via paper certification.
SML requires E&O with a coverage minimum of $1M.  We do request renewed certificates of coverage upon expiration.
Copies of E&O coverage is required, $1 Million min.
Only require the General Agent to hold E&O

E&O is required for all products except Medicare Supplement, Final Expense, Accidental Death, Children's Whole Life, and Term Life Express. $1,000,000 coverage per claim is the required minimum.  Banks and BDs are $5,000,000.

data.Describe login process for client & agen:
DocuSign:


    
	GA: Use the ID and password provided by iPipeline.
    
	Producer: Use the ID and password provided by iPipeline.
    
	Owner/Insured: Use last 4 digits of their SSN followed by their date of birth. Then follow with two factor authentication, either by phone or text message a code is provided for them to enter.


DocFast: 


    
	GA: Use last 4 digits of the owner's SSN. 
    
	Producer: Use last 4 digits of the owner's SSN.

data.e-Delivery Platform/Portal: www.My.SBLI.com for SBLI policies.  www.MyCentrian.com for Centrian Life (SBLI subsidiary in Connecticut).
data.e-Delivery Workflow: At time of application, the agent or client can enroll in the e-Delivery.  At issue, an e-mail notification is automatically sent to the client (including upsell options, if eligible) and any outstanding forms or amendments can be electronically signed and paid for at www.My.SBLI.com or www.MyCentrian.com.  Proactive alert notifications on the application's current status are available to keep you updated.  Make sure you have your alerts switched on via www.sbliagent.com.
data.Describe login process for client & agen: New clients (First Time User) will be asked to register online (Register Now).  The client will be asked to enter: Policy Owner name, Tax ID or Social Security number, ZIP/Postal Code, Policy Number.  The client will be asked to create a Username and Password.
data.When does policy auto-release to next co: After a policy has been approved, as applied for, by the SBLI Underwriters, the policy will be Issued and an e-mail notification will automatically be sent to the client (including upsell options, if eligible) and any outstanding forms or amendments can be electronically signed and paid for at www.My.SBLI.com.
data.When will a paper policy be generated?: If the Insured opts out of the e-Delivery process.  If the Insured and Owner are not the same person. Conversion policies.
data.Limitations / Exclusions: If the Insured opts out of the e-Delivery process.  If the Insured and Owner are not the same person. Conversion policies.
data.How to opt-in for e-Delivery?: BGA opts-in to e-Delivery to make available for clients. At time of application, the client can elect e-Delivery.
data.Can this be done for all policies or cas: If BGA opts-in to e-Delivery, all policies will have the option to elect e-Delivery, except If the Insured opts out of the e-Delivery process.  If the Insured and Owner are not the same person. Conversion policies.
data.Payment options available via e-Delivery: SBLI policy owners can pay their premiums via EFT. Funds for premiums can be withdrawn from either a statement savings or a checking account.  SBLI accepts MasterCard or Visa credit card payments for premiums.  The initial payment made by credit card is free, any additional premiums paid by credit card can be paid for via our customer portal, www.My.SBLI.com.  Premiums paid by credit card outside of the initial premium will be subject to a transaction processing fee by a 3rd party payment processor of $3.95 for each policy premium.

data.e-Delivery Workflow: All signers will receive an email with a secure link. Policy is delivered to the agent (and reviewer if reviewer was requested) concurrently. Once agent signs (and reviewer signs off), link will be sent to the client (insured/owner)to sign. If Insured and Owner are the same person, one link will be sent. If Insured and Owner are different, link will be sent to each concurrently. Once client (Insured/Owner) signs, delivery documents will be sent to Securian Financial for review.
data.Describe login process for client & agen: To access the policy and documents contained in the link, each signer (agent and insured/owner) must authenticate themselves using a four-digit PIN that will be either the last four digits of the signer's Social Security number (SSN) or Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN). If an ePolicy Delivery case manager has been added, their PIN will be the first four digits of your Securian Financial life insurance code.
data.When does policy auto-release to next co: Once agent signs (and reviewer signs off), link will be sent to the client (insured/owner)to sign. If Insured and Owner are the same person, one link will be sent. If Insured and Owner are different, link will be sent to each concurrently.Once client (Insured/Owner) signs, delivery documents will be sent to Securian Financial for review.
data.When will a paper policy be generated?: The DocuSign email link is good for 60 calendar days. If the link expires- we will mail the paper policy 5 days thereafter.
data.Limitations / Exclusions: E-Delivery is not available for NY. Certain delivery requirements may cause the policy to be ineligible. There is no notification to you or your clients when a policy is ineligible. Ineligible policies are sent via paper and the standard delivery method.
data.How to opt-in for e-Delivery?: E-Delivery is available on applications that are submitted via E-App. (in all states except NY).This can be opted in at time of application or before issue.
data.Can this be done for all policies or cas: E-Delivery is not available for NY. Certain delivery requirements may cause the policy to be ineligible. There is no notification to you or your clients when a policy is ineligible. Ineligible policies are sent via paper and the standard delivery method.
data.Payment options available via e-Delivery: Electronic Payment Authorization Form
data.Can premium mode be changed during e-Del: We recommend that you reach out to the Home Office (Case Manager) to update the premium mode.

data.e-Delivery Workflow: Client and Case Manager are sent the E-Delivery link the next day the policy is issued within NB. If needing a PDF please download from TransACT this is for all fixed life products currently sold.
data.Describe login process for client & agen: Client Sender will be New Business (policydelivery@ipipeline.com), Subject line Your Insurance policy is ready, policy owner enters last four of SSN to review the policy and to E-sign then if no signature is needed the owner will click accept or if signature is needed they will sign via Docu-Sign and the requirements are automatically sent back to TA for placement. No Agent signature is required.
data.When will a paper policy be generated?: 20 days after the policy issue date.
data.Limitations / Exclusions:
New York, Guam, Puerto Rico, or Virgin Islands

NOTE: Not available in Maryland when policy is

approved less than applied with sub-standard risk class

• Insured or owner is not a U.S. resident

• Insured is a juvenile

• Organizations, trusts, or multiple owners

• Owner/insured email address and/or SSN missing

• Payor is other than insured or owner

• EFT delivery requirement is present

• Illustrations signed on delivery

• Policy reissues

• Any pre-issue requirements that are granted

an exception to obtain on delivery

We will need to complete the eConsent 2017 and eDelivery Terms and Conditions.

data.Can this be done for all policies or cas: This would be available for all Term and FFIUL, but depending on requirements and other knockouts this would be a case by case.
data.Payment options available via e-Delivery: The payment forms can be completed through Doc-Fast but no payment taken.
data.Can premium mode be changed during e-Del: Payment mode can be changed at anytime but if switching from Direct Bill to EFT; the EFT form would be completed outside of Doc-Fast.

data.Are alerts sent when posted and sent to: A final completion email is sent to BGA when policy is delivered/completed. BGA will continue to receive final inforce notices from their CLIC case manager once the policy has been placed.

Our platform can only support a single email address per BGA.  If BGAs ask that epolicies route through their office as the first point of contact - they can later release the link to the agent.

data.Are alerts sent when posted and sent to: Progress can always be tracked on our edelivery platform.  Users will be able to see which party is currently in possession of the link and whether it has been completed.  Updates will also be posted to our Producer Bay website.

data.Who receives POS notices?: Clients are alerted of the approval of policies and the e-Delivery process by email.  If the client does not register and accept their new policy a second reminder email will be sent two weeks later.
data.What notices are sent?: An initial email is sent to the client within a day of the policy being approved.  The email notifies the client of the approval and provides instructions to register the new policy and retrieve a copy.  A second email is sent two weeks, if the client doesn't respond to the first email, reminding the client to register and accept the policy.
data.How are notices sent?: Notices are sent via email to the client.
data.Are documents available online?: Documents, such as Application Amendments, Good Health Statements, Policy Delivery Receipt, and payment forms are available online, as part of the e-Delivery process.
data.Are alerts sent when posted and sent to: Alerts for agents are posted online through www.SBLIAgent.com on each client's/application's Policy Detail page.  Alerts can also be emailed to agents by utilization of the enhanced communications functions in www.SBLIAgent.com.

data.Who receives POS notices?: Signers receive an email from Minnesota Life PolicyDeliveryTeam@Securian.com through DocuSign with a link to review and sign. Emails are first sent to the financial professional and reviewer concurrently. Once signatures are complete emails are then sent to the insured and policy owner concurrently.
data.What notices are sent?: Reminder emails are sent after five days, then every three days. The link is valid for 60 days. Expiration warning email is sent one day before the link expires.
data.How are notices sent?: Once the link expires, a notification will be sent to the communication contact on the case, informing them the Link was voided and policy will be mailed on paper in 5 business days.
data.Are documents available online?: If sent via e-delivery, Policy PDF and the Signed Policy Packet is available for download via Docusign. The Policy PDF or signed Policy Packet is also available on our website - https://advisor.securian.com/documents
data.Are alerts sent when posted and sent to: Once Link expires, a notification will be sent to the communication contact informing them the Link was voided and policy will be mailed on paper in 5 business days. An email through DocuSign is also sent to the signers once the link has voided.

data.Are alerts sent when posted and sent to: An email is sent with the pdf copy of the policy and it is delivered to the BGA office (main email address listed in our files) as well as to the agent and to the client.  If you prefer us not to send any pdf copies of polices out, you do have the option to opt out of our current pdf policy process. Please contact your Sales Director or Account Executive to opt out.

data.Online Payments: Customers can register to pay online by setting up an account profile at https://www.bannerlife.com/
data.Other Ways to Pay: Speech-driven payment system, powered by SmartAction. Customers can pay premiums using 800-638-8428 toll-free number for Banner and 800-346-4773 for William Penn. Customers can also opt-in for text reminders, receive bill notifications, past due notifications, and have the ability to pay their premium via text message. App payments: Policyowners or payors will need to add the LGA pass to the wallet app on their smart phones. There are two apps; one for iPhones (Apple Wallet) and one for Androids (Google Pay). Digital wallet payment is not available on an iPad.

data.Online Payments: Policyholders may make payments online at cinfin.com using the $Pay Now button.
Yes
Corebridge Financial has the option to pay premiums online via their consumer portal. Visit corebridgefinancial.com/lifeportal

For Electronic Payments We offer two convenient electronic payment methods: Automatic, recurring payments and Online, one-time payments via John Hancock ePay (Please note: options cannot be used for initial premium payments on newly issued policies)  Customers can get started with either option by visiting johnhancock.com/lifepayment. data.Rules & Compensation: When an in-force policy (life or annuity), is replaced or if its benefits are reduced in conjunction with the issuance of a new policy, it is considered an internal replacement. Partial or total surrenders, lapses with or without value, decreases in benefit amounts, or loans in excess of 25 percent of all applicable policy loan values are all considered a reduction in coverage for replacement purposes. If the activity takes place within six months before or after the date of application or effective date of the new policy, it is considered a replacement. Policies eligible for conversion are excluded from this definition. Commissions may be reduced on new policies that are replacing existing policies. Existing policies include any with the Company or its subsidiaries. Commissions may also be reduced when the applicant is deemed to have a replacement history. In situations where an agent other than the one who wrote the original policy writes the replacement, the new agent writing the case receives all applicable compensation and production credit for the case subject to any replacement adjustment.

Will pay full commissions after the 1st policy year if the replacement is in the best interest of the policyowner/insured and all sutability and replacement requirements have been met. No chargebacks after 90 days.
N/A

After underwriter approval, cases with internal replacements are current dated.  We will require a Policy Cancellation and Disbursement Request Form to complete the internal replacement.  This form can be obtained on delivery.  Once all delivery requirements are received and the case in placed inforce, POS is notified to complete the internal replacement.  If the form indicates funds from the existing policy will be applied to the new policy, then the new policy will be placed inforce once those funds are received from POS.
Term to Term > or = 8 yrs - Full First Year Sales Credit (FYSC)
Term to Term < 8 yrs - FYSC on increase in annual premium
Term to Perm < 1yr - FYSC on increase in annual premium
Term to Perm > 1yr - FYSC
Perm to Term < 10yrs - Full FYSC on any increase in annual premium
Perm to Term > 10yrs - FYSC
Perm to Perm < 10yrs - No credits on rolled values; FYSC on increase in annual (target) premium
Perm to Perm > 10yrs - Full FYSC on rolled values; FYSC on new money
Annuity to Perm - No credits on rolled values; FYSC on new money

Please refer to Reference materials: Internal Replacement Guidelines and the New York - Internal Replacement Guidelines - available on JHSalesHub.comFor further inquiries - please contact National Sales Support Team at: 888-266-7498, option 2.

An Internal Replacement for these purposes is defined as:   Any life insurance coverage on the life of an insured, originally issued by Lincoln or Jefferson Pilot (or theiraffiliates) that is reduced in the face amount or terminated (lapsed or surrendered) and replaced with anyother form of Lincoln life coverage on the same insured for an equal, lower or increased face amount. If awithdrawal, surrender or loan of the policy values from an existing policy is used to pay premiums on thenew policy on the same insured within 4 months before the issuance of the new policy or 13 months afterthe issuance of the new policy, i.e. a “financial purchase”, it will be considered an internal replacement andcompensation will be adjusted accordingly. Refer to the Compensation on Financial Transactions examples.For policies with the Supplemental Term Insurance Rider (both Primary and Other Insured versions), if thebase policy lapses within 4 months before the Rider conversion or 13 months after the Rider conversion,the rider conversion will be treated as an Internal Replacement.• The definition encompasses both 1035 (tax advantaged) and non-1035 exchanges.• This definition encompasses replacements involving both single life and survivorship policies, including asingle life policy being replaced by a survivorship policy.• If the policy owner changes (including cases involving Trusts) but at least one of the insured remains thesame, the transaction will be handled as an internal replacement.• If different agents/brokers are involved in the transaction, but the insured(s) remain the same, thetransaction will be handled as an internal replacement._______General Compensation Information:• If a new policy is issued without underwriting, as part of a contractually guaranteed exchange provision in a policy,no first-year compensation will be paid on the new policy.• If the internal replacement includes a loan rollover, then only the NET amount gets any compensation except asnoted below. Please note that loans cannot be rolled over into the MoneyGuard® series.• If the old policy is replaced at any time within its commission recall period, 100% chargeback will be applied to theold policy regardless of the regular product chargeback schedule. Full compensation will be paid on the new policy.Refer to the Commission Recall (Chargeback) Guidelines for information on the commission recall period since itvaries by product type.• If a policy is replacing a policy that is 16 or more years old, full compensation on rollover and new money will bepaid and it will be treated as new business by our Inforce Underwriting team (formally known as TCXN); however,replacement paperwork will be required. Rollover loans will be fully compensated.• If the internal replacement involves more than one policy with durations less than 16 years and another with aduration more than 16 years, please refer to the example.• If a transaction is considered to be part of an internal replacement, the rules considered the COMBINED targetamount being replaced, no matter how many new policies are involved. However, replacement paperwork (basedon regulatory requirements) would only need to be submitted for any policy actually receiving replacement money.• See Life Product Internal Replacement Compensation Examples for sample calculations.

At Application  If the agent knows the client wishes to do an internal replacement (as defined above) the replacement questions on the application must be admitted, meaning answered in the affirmative and the application must be identified as a replacement when submitted. The first-year commission will be automatically adjusted for the replacement. All new policy applications must identify which polic(ies) are being replaced, including term conversions when other coverage (besides the amount of term being converted) will also be surrendered or lapsed. Example: A new $500,000 Whole Life policy is applied for that will be issued by converting an existing $500,000 Term policy. If the client also surrenders an existing VUL policy at the same time, that makes this Whole Life purchase a replacement since other coverage is being terminated.  After Issue  If the new insurance was not identified as a replacement prior to issue, but an internal replacement as defined above occurs in the thirteen (13) months preceding or the thirteen (13) months following the application date of the new policy, the new policy will be handled as an internal replacement, and new replacement forms must be submitted to the Home Office for processing. If it is discovered that an existing MassMutual policy has lapsed, surrendered, or has been terminated in any way, within 13 months of the signing of the application, the new policy or contract will be treated as an internal replacement.  INTERNAL REPLACEMENT OF LIFE INSURANCE – ADJUSTMENTS TO FYC First-year commissions will be modified for all permanent life insurance policies and riders meeting the above definition of internal replacement. Thirty-five percent (35%) of normal first-year commissions will be paid on the premium attributable to the replaced face amount. For example, if a new policy that normally receives a 55 percent commission is sold as an internal replacement, 35% of 55% (19.3%) will be paid on the replacement amount. Full commissions will be paid on the premiums attributable to any increase in the basic face amount above the original amount of coverage. 4 of 16 Please note that for all non-traditional and applicable traditional life products, the reduced commission rate (35% of normal) will be paid on both target and excess premium. Please also note that ALIR and LISR premiums always receive 2% commissions, even in a replacement situation.  Allowances are payable on the reduced commission, not the normal commission. Overrides are also payable at 35% of the normal override rate for policy years 1 and 2. First-year commissions will be modified for all term policies meeting the definition of internal replacement in Part 1. Replacement commissions equal to the renewal rate will be paid on the premium attributable to the replaced face amount. Full commissions will be paid on the premium attributable to any increase in the basic face amount above the original amount of coverage  Replacement Policy Effective Prior to the 1st Anniversary Date of Original Policy Commissions on any unearned premium are charged back for the months the original policy is no longer in force. Commissions for internal replacements are calculated as follows: The first year commission payable for the replacement policy depends on the number of months of first year premium and commission already paid on the original policy. 1.	Determine the number of months of premium and commission paid on the original policy. For example, if the original policy was issued with a face amount of $150,000 on 8/1/2018 and a replacement policy issued 4/1/2019, 8 months of premium and commission were paid on the original policy. 2.	Divide the $150,000 face amount by 12 (months) and then multiply by the months inforce to get the replaced face amount. In the above example, $100,000 face amount will be payable at replacement first-year commission rates and 4 months of face amount, or $50,000, will be payable at the full first-year commission rate In these instances, the general agent or general manager and the agent will be notified and the agent’s first-year commission will be adjusted via a charge-back.
Yes, request internal exchange form

Internal replacements are non-commissionable.  We do not pay comp on any 1035 exchange money with regards to an internal exchange.  We only pay compensation on new premium applied to the newer policy.   Internal replacement rules:  - Insured on both the new and old policy must be the same  - Term conversions are not considered an internal exchange  - A new surrender charge period will begin on the new policy  - A new completed application must be submitted for all cases  - For policies inforce for 5 or more years, a new application (including the applicable medical questions) and MIB information

An exchange is defined as changing from one North American life policy to another North American policy with underwriting. Money coming from the internal exchange is considered “rollover” money and will be split into two categories to determine compensation. Rollover/gain (internal money) commissions are paid by policy year: •	Years 1–10: 0% •	Year 11: 20% •	Year 12: 40% •	Year 13: 60% •	Year 14: 80% •	Year 15+: 100%.   All external 1035 money is 100% commissionable.
See Replacement Guidelines: https://oasf.my.salesforce.com/sfc/p/50000000bbUu/a/TO0000017h2r/pvUtPxGsXVIoqAEe6sI8LoxvuqyEtP_75EJ3qaH4MBU

Promise Term - Renewal commissions are payable on internal replacement of the Company's policies and on replacements of policies issued by other Pacific Life Companies only to the extent that there is no increased coverage provided by the replacing policy. If the replacing policy provides increased coverage, first-year commissions are payable on the amount of increased coverage.  Surrender charges are not waived if a policy issued by the company or other Pacific Life company is replaced.  First-year commissions paid for increased replacement coverage are eligible for bonus.     	Promise Conversion UL and Guaranteed UL - The target premium for an internal replacement will be adjusted based on the waived surrender charge from the replaced policy.  Excess premiums will not be adjusted.  Commissions paid on target premium for the internal replacement are eligible for bonus.  Adjusted Target = Original target (-) waived surrender charge of the replaced policy.

Rules - Owner must be the same on the old and new policies  Comp - Please see the Comp Schedule for the official details (DD715) We pay on the difference of the new premium minus the old premium up to target

Visit the https://www.myprotective.com/Working-with-Protective/Doing-Business-with-Us/Policy-Revisions/ on our website for details.  Rules vary depending on product that is being replaced.  Always best to call 877-778-3500 and verify what the replacement rules are for the policy in question.
Please see the Pru Highlighter,  "How Prudential Applies Replacement Compensation Rules" for details

An internal exchange occurs when an SBLI life insurance policy that has been in force for less than 8 years is lapsed or surrendered within one year of the issuance of another SBLI life insurance policy for that same insured.  A life insurance policy conversion processed by contractual right is not an internal exchange.  For an internal exchange to a new policy having a greater premium than the replaced policy, subtract the premium on the replaced policy from the premium on the new policy and then multiply by the applicable percentage, according to the SBLI Commission Schedule for the commission on the new policy.  For an internal exchange where the new premium is equal to or less than the premium on the replaced policy, no commission is payable.

Internal replacements are allowed; however it is not alace  commissionable event. Polices that are 10 years or older are eligible for an internal replacement to a new product, which waives surrender charges and underwriting.Internal Replacement is the purchase of a new Minnesota Life contract to repan existing Minnesota Life contact(s). To be processed as an internal replacement, the "old" contract had to have lapsed or been surrendered within the past six months.

Compliance with State Regulations are required (replacement paperwork).  Existing policy should be kept in force to ensure coverage.  At the time of medical approval an effective date for the new policy and values will be obtained from our Customer Service Department.  Replaced policy is not terminated until all delivery requirements have been received for the new policy and  the new policy is ready to be placed in force.

Term to Term Replacements:  • We require a new application and underwriting is required along with the state replacement requirements. • For policies in force for 0-5 Years, commission paid at 50% of the commission rate. • For policies in force for 6+ Years commission paid at 100% of the commission rate.  UL to UL Replacements:   We require a new application and underwriting along with all state replacement requirements. • For policies in force for 0-7 Years, commissions paid on the increase in commissionable target premium only • For policies in force for 8+ Years, commissions paid at 100% of the commissionable target premium

For internal replacements it would be best to contact commissions with the inforce policy number to confirm the commissions schedule as it could vary depending upon what is being replaced and the time frame being inforce.

A replacement is changing polices within the same product type. A replacement can be from a term policy to a term policy, Universal Life to Universal Life, Term policy to a Universal life (If not exercising conversion rights), and from Universal Life to Term.   Compensation is paid only on the increase of premium from the old plan to the new plan.
