data.Cash with App / TIA / conditional recei: John Hancock uses a TIA that provides 90 days of temporary coverage which is equal to the lesser of the amount applied for or $1,000,000 individual and $5,000,000 survivorship.  The TIA is an agreement separate from the insurance contract.  Money will be accepted only with the application and a completed TIA or upon delivery.  The following are our guideline for collecting the money as well as providing coverage under a TIA:   - All questions on the TIA application must be answered ""No""  - Insured age is 20-70 years  - The total amount of life insurance being applied for does not exceed $10,000,000 individual or $15,000,000 survivorship  - The TIA conditions must be satisfied, including submission of at least 1/12th of the annual premium or the product being applied for.For Reference materials: Please refer to John Hancock's Temporary Insurance Agreement - "lock it in" - available on JHSalesHub.com
data.Acceptable Forms of Payment: - Cashiers check - must state ""Cashier's Check"" - Initial payment - OK  - EFT - Initial payment is OK regardless of mode        - The Request for Pre-Authorization Payment Plan form (NB5087) is required with a voided check        - If automatic bank draft is selected for the initial premium payment, it must be selected as the preferred premium payment method for all premiums (not available for TIA)  - Other        Owner's / Payor's personal check - must be original and payable to John Hancock - OK for initial payment        Wire transfers - Please Note: e-Payment is available for Inforce policies, only and available for most products.For Reference materials: Please refer to How to submit initial premium - available on JHSalesHub.com
data.Wire Instructions/Details: Please refer to 'Wire transfer banking' .pdf available on JHSalesHub.com

data.Cash with App / TIA / conditional recei: TIA for Temporary Insurance Agreement (TIA) coverage for paper Term applications and Permanent products, the dates to be compared are the application/ticket signature date and the TIA signature date. Most states require the TIA signature date to be within 7 days (no more than 10 days for MoneyGuard) of the application/ticket signature date or the premium has to be refunded. The following states allow 30 days between the app signed date ant the TIA signed date: MO, VA (includes MG) and NJ.  TIA can be taken for application amounts up to and including $3 Million of coverage and the maximum amount of coverage is $500,000 for all applications under the terms of the TIA. Coverage terminates with a 90 day maximum. See TIA for specific Terms and Conditions. If premium has been received  but the TIA is not in good order or was not received, we will request the TIA at time of coding. The TIA will then need to be returned IGO within 7 days from notification.  TIA is not accepted in Kansas.Temporary insurance is available with TermAccel and LincXpress.  Effective 11/15/21 a new TIA form is to be used that does not require advance payment to put the TIA into effect.  The client must instead agree to a stated amount (equal to the first modal premium) to be deducted from the death benefit upon death of the Proposed Insured per the terms and conditions as stated on the form.Advance payment will continue to be required for Permanent products and Term full applications using Form LFF11524 (state variations)

data.Acceptable Forms of Payment: SBLI policy owners can pay their premiums via Electronic Funds Transfers (EFT), required for monthly premium mode.  Funds for premiums can be withdrawn from either a statement savings or a checking account.  SBLI accepts MasterCard or Visa credit card payments for premiums, except for monthly premium mode. The initial payment made by credit card is free, any additional premiums paid by credit card can be paid for via our customer portal, www.My.SBLI.com.  Premiums paid by credit card outside of the initial premium will be subject to a transaction processing fee by a 3rd party payment processor of $3.95 for each policy premium.  Clients can also pay their premiums by check.  Cashier checks are accepted up to a maximum of $3,000 for initial and one-time premium payments.  Money orders are accepted up to a maximum of $3,000 for initial and one-time premium payments.

data.Acceptable Forms of Payment: You can choose to pay on a monthly, quarterly, semi-annual or annual basis to suit your needs. Depending on your premium amount, you also have the option to pay your initial premium by credit card if is $2,000 or less, or via Automated Clearing House (ACH), a network that transfers money between banks. (ACH payments typically come from checking accounts. If you want yours to come from a savings account, we recommend asking your bank first, as not all banks accommodate this request.) Recurring premiums are automatically drafted via ACH at the frequency that was selected during the application process: annually (one yearly payment), semi-annually (two payments—every 6 months), quarterly (four payments—every 3 months), or monthly (twelve payments—1 per month).  You can increase or decrease your premium payments at any time to better fit your current financial needs. As long as you make sufficient premium payments to cover the Lapse Protection Benefit requirements, your insurance coverage will remain in effect. Contact your insurance professional to discuss the implications of changes in premium payments.

data.Procedures: Unless save age is requested, all policies are current dated through our Horizon Digital Application process upon issue to the client. Save Age feature can be requested through Horizon via our self service tool or via communication to our New Business team.

Unless otherwise requested, policy is dated seven days beyond approval date. Will backdate up to six months unless state regulations dictate otherwise. Internal replacements will be dated  to coordinate with the day of the month of the existing policy.
Yes - we have specific dates on when we can issue/activate a policy

We advance date all term cases 21 days (non CA) and 30 days for CA unless a specific policy date is provided. If client wishes to save age, we can do that provided the state allows – most we can go back up to 6 months.  The state of OH only allows up to 3 months.  For UL and VUL we current date unless a different date is provided.

In general, the policy date is set to the current issue date except for the following scenarios: 1) Backdate to save age request is received a. Backdating must be reflected on the illustration b. Completed Notice - Backdating / Special Dating form (NB0025) must be submitted 2) Cash on Delivery (COD) - the policy date will be set to the date that the first minimum initial premium is received Note: The Policy date is not permitted to fall on the 29th , 30th, or 31st of the month.; 3) Internal 1035 Exchange – the policy date will be the surrender date of the policy that was exchanged; Backdating is an option 4) External 1035 + Internal 1035 – the policy date will be the Internal surrender date of the of the policy that was exchanged; Backdating is an option.5) Term Conversions – Please contact the Term Conversion Team. Please Note: Winflex also does not illustrate backdating well with John Hancock policies, so you can call our Sales Desk at 888-266-7498, option 2.For Reference materials: Please refer to Guidelines to Issue Date, Policy Date and Policy Backdating- available on JHSalesHub.com

Unless saving age (policy is dated 6 months and 3 days from insured’s DOB for Life policies only - See below for MG), policies are dated current at issue and then re-dated as of the date we review the final requirements with the exception of VUL which is dated within 2 days of receipt of the final requirements (Except for MoneyGuard where the policy date matches the date Lincoln received the initial premium due but no more than 1 month after the policy date). A policy date endorsement is sent out when the date is changed  For MoneyGuard, the client’s age is locked in upon submission of the ticket or the signature date of the Part 1 application. At issue and placement, as long as the clients age has not changed, we would date the policy the date all placement restrictive requirements were reviewed and deemed In Good Order.

The date of issue is the policy effective date unless the BGA requests backdating to save age (up to 6 months). The policy can be future dated up to 2 weeks upon request. NOTE: For backdating, MassMutual uses age nearest/insurance age and not actual age.
We date policies the date they are issued unless we are saving age
Typically date current or can date to save age or request certain preference
Typically date current or can date to save age or request certain preference
Typically date current or can date to save age or request certain preference

OneAmerica dates the policy the date premium is received in the Home Office unless we are saving age. If we are saving age, OneAmerica dates the policy the day prior to the insured(s) birthday. Indexed Annuity Care policies are always dated 14th of the month.

Backdate Notice - The Backdate Notice allows a policy to have an earlier policy date, because the policy date determines when premiums are due. Many policyholders can save money if backdating gives them a younger issue age. Backdate Notices generate as Delivery Requirements at issue any time the policy is dated prior to the Issue Date. Policy Delivery Acknowledgement - Policy Delivery Acknowledgements (PDAs) go out in all policies except those that are backdated, including internal replacements, 1035, Save Age, and Special Dating.      	The Short Form PDA advises the Policy Owner (PO) that the date the form is signed will become the policy date. 	    The Long Form PDA generates whenever the PI's Save Age date falls within the delivery period. The Policy Owner has the option of keeping the Save Age date (the policy will be effective for either the Save Age date or the signed date, whichever is earliest) or having the policy reissued with the policy date as the PDA signed date.

If initial premium was submitted with the application, the policy date will be the date we issue the policy unless backdating to save age has been requested.  We use Age Nearest Birthday to calculate age and can backdate up to 6 months from the signature date.  If the policy is COD, we will re-date the policy to the date of the contractual delivery requirements.  **Note, we do not date policies in advance nor do we date a policy on the 29th, 30th or 31st, these policies will be dated to the 1st.

For paper policies (unless Saving Age):    Effective date advanced 30 days from issue for policies without money applied    Effective date advanced 15 days from issue for policies with money applied / authorization to draft initial premium    For electronic policies (unless Saving Age):            Effective date advanced 15 days from issue        Save Age:                    	 Up to 180 days from application date            	If the billing mode is Annual of Semi-Annual, we will Save Age automatically if an age change occurred in the current or previous month.                        	If the billing mode is Quarterly or Monthly, we will only Save Age upon request
See Bulletin, "Simple Rules for Dating Prudential Policies Flyer"

SBLI will allow on an application "backdate" to save age.  In general, it is an age within six months of the last birthday; however, please consult with SBLI regarding the state of issue as this request can be subject to state regulatory requirements.

Policy date: the actual date written on the life insurance contract. The policy date is important because it dictates the policy anniversaries, policy years, policy months and premium due dates. The policy date also represents the beginning of the contestable period. How policy date is determined: The policy date is determined when the policy is requested. This is typically around the time of Underwriting Decision unless there are outstanding requirements necessary before the policy can be issued or mailed.  Backdating a policy date: Occasionally, a policy date is needed that precedes the date coverage was approved, such as when a client's age changes. Back premiums must be collected to put the policy in force. Note: SecureCare policies can only be backdated up to 30 days from underwriting decision. Most other policies can be backdated six months prior to the application date. Save Age: Age is determined by the client's nearest birthday. Once a client is more than six months into their current age, the insurance age increases by one year. Save age is when the policy issue date selected is prior to the date the client's insurance age changed. When a policy is requested, if the insured's date of birth is within the next 28 days, the policy is dated to save age. Note: SecureCare policies can only be backdated up to 30 days from underwriting decision. This product is issued using the insured's actual age instead of insurance age.  Advanced dating: Special requests to advance date a policy must be clearly stated on the application. Policies can be prepared with issue dates of up to 11 months in advance. Advance dated policies cannot be put inforce until three weeks prior to the effective date. Money should not be collected if the advance issue date will be more than 3 weeks past the date you expect the underwriting decision.

Unless saving age or a date is specified, the policy effective date will be the date the policy is issued.  You can save age (6 months & 3 days from the birthdate) or specify a date within 6 months from the application date.  Ohio only allows backdating up to 3 months.
Policies are dated the day that the policy is issued unless backdating to save age.Non Swift Term product (Core Products) We will not issue using the days of the 29th, 30th and 31st.
Swift Products does not allow backdating.

As a general rule, of any month. current date policies unless we are saving age or 1035 is involved.  If 1035 is involved, we date the policy the day the check was issued.  We cannot date a policy the 28th, 29th, 30th or the 31st
For Life Insurance polices we current date policies at the time of underwriting approval, unless backdating to save age.

We support both an automated feed via FTP for an interested office or the distributor can come into our website and request the download.  Both XML and Excel formats are supported.
data.Can BGA control producers access to webs: Yes, we do allow a producer to register online (TransAct) but they are given basic access which is exclusive to software, commission statements and sales / marketing materials.  The GA must request / approve access to pending or inforce.

data.Contact for Expedited Requests/Problems: Lisa Hunter, Manager Policyowner Operations  Ph 402-351-7039 lisa.hunter@mutualofomaha.com.  Requests can also be sent to our life.service@mutualofomaha.com general email box.
data.Inforce Data on website: We do have inforce information that you can look up by individual policy on our  Sales Professional Access website. data.Inforce Data feed capabilities: Yes, contact your Life Sales Director or Account Executive for additional details and set up
data.Online Inforce info for producers: On our Sales Professional Access, the producer has the ability to review policy information by policy number, last 4 digits of a social security number or name. The ability to pull  lists is unavailable - it is by individual policy only.

APPS and ExamOne have implemented guidelines that restrict firms from ordering exam requirements billed to the Prudential account for cases ages 18-60, and up to $3M in face amount.Prudential handles the ordering medical requirements for cases with face amounts of $3M and below and ages 18 – 60 in order to maximize the number of cases that are assessed through PruFast Track. PruFast Track provides a faster, more convenient underwriting experience for eligible applicants.What does this mean for this segment of cases?    Prudential orders all requirements (even if the agent report or cover sheet indicates otherwise)    Exam/ APS orders for Fast Track eligible cases are not allowed under a Prudential billing account with APPS and ExamOne    If requirements are provided with the application, please contact the Prudential case management team to have Prudential's order cancelled.    For applications requesting coverage amounts above $3 million, or for proposed insureds over the age of 60 or under 18, the BGA/Producer may order medical requirements, or Prudential is available to order requirements as well. Please indicate the ordering preference on the agents report.

data.Other Company Exams: Banner Life will use another company's exams however it is subject to exam shelf life and underwriter approval. We may still require our Non-Med in addition to the exam.  Exam Shelf Life: Permissible time limits for routine age and amount medical evidence, where the results are normal applies to abbreviated, paramedical and medical exams, resting/ treadmill electrocardiogram, blood/urine:  Ages 20 - 60: 1 year, Ages 61 - 80: 6 months, Ages over 80: 3 months.
data.When does UW review the case?: The underwriter reviews the case after the case manager has completed the initial review.  This case is usually with the underwriter for review within 24-48 hours from receipt of the application.
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: The client now has access to an online portal through the lab vendor to log in and obtain their lab results.  Banner Life no longer mails them to the client.
data.If yes, how can clients obtain lab resul: The client now has access to an online portal through the lab vendor to log in and obtain their lab results.  Banner Life no longer mails them to the client.

data.Other Company Exams: Able to use other carrier's exam forms. Will require a "statement in leiu of" when delivering the policy.
data.When does UW review the case?: Placed in underwriting queue immediately upon issueance of policy number.
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: Insured receives retrieval information when completing the exam. Results are obtained directly from ExamOne, www.myexamone.com/access-my-results/.

data.Other Company Exams: Yes, if not completed in NY and not older than 12 months.
data.When does UW review the case?: No later than day 3 of application being received
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: Yes if requested
data.If yes, how can clients obtain lab resul: Agent cannot see, Client can request

data.Other Company Exams: We can use another carriers exam, provided we get a fully completed Corebridge Financial Part B for the correct state. Those are good for up to one year but depends on the insured’s age and underwriters discretion.
data.When does UW review the case?: For some non-Express cases, there is an automated component that reviews some requirements before the  case is sent for an intial review by the underwriter.  For AUS, all age and amount requirements must be received as well as the agent licensing, before it is processed in the automated underwriting program.
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: We can release the Lab results out but it is done through our dedicated vendors. Insured just has to complete a Labcard and the vendor will send directly to them.  CRL will now releases the lab results on a secure website.  An instructional card is given to the applicant at the time of the exam.

data.Other Company Exams: We will accept another company's paramedical exam.  However, a John Hancock Health Questionnaire must also be included if another company's paramedical exam is submitted.For Reference materials: Please refer to Field Underwriting Guide and Routine Underwriting Requirements - available on JHSalesHub.com
data.When does UW review the case?: Initial underwriting assessment within 5 business days from receipt of application. NOTE: a final offer will be provided upon full review of all requested medical records, all age and amount underwriting and administrative requirements and a complete John Hancock Application.For Reference materials: Please refer to Service standards - available on JHSalesHub.com
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: Yes.  ExamOne (LabOne) and Clinical Reference Library (CRL) both provide easy access to lab results.
data.Are labs results available? Yes / No: Yes. ExamOne (LabOne) and Clinical Reference Library (CRL) both provide easy access to lab results.
data.If yes, how can clients obtain lab resul:
1. ExamOne (LabOne): 
The Examiner provides the client with a brochure that includes a unique bar code sticker. The client follows the instructions on the brochure to obtain the results. LabOne sends an email to the client when results are available to view. Note: Labs are available within 7-14 days of the time the exam was completed and will remain available online for six months. The client logs in and receives a greeting letter and the lab report.

2. Clinical Reference Library (CRL)

The Examiner provides the client a Slip ID containing a unique bar code sticker. The client adds the slip ID to the last 4 digits of his/her phone number as entered on the consent form. The client follows the instructions on the Slip ID to access the labs. Note: Lab results are available within 14 days from the time the exam was completed and remain online for six months. The client logs in and receives a greeting letter and the lab report.

data.Other Company Exams: We will use them as long as the appropriate information is included (Senior Supplement at applicable ages) and the Non-Med/Part II is fully completed.
data.When does UW review the case?: For traditional full paper applications and eApp's, once a case is coded in the system it is referred to the Underwriter for initial review.  For LincXpress Tele App business, the case is not referred to the underwriter until after the phone interview is completed.  Each time a underwriting requirement is sub mitted the case is sent to the Underwriter for review.    LincXpress Tele App (Term and Permanent) and TermAccel  business where insured's age is 18-60 and the face amount is $2.5M or less qualify for Automated UW and Lab Free processing
data.Lab Results to the Proposed Insured: We no longer routinely send a copy of the lab results and exam with the policy after a case is issued. Clients are now able to access their lab results online at CRL and ExamOne websites. However, there are times when a proposed insured is unable to access their labs online and would want a copy of the labs if the case has been declined or withdrawn and in some instances, still pending.  Any request for copies of lab results and/or exams will be referred to the underwriter.  This request may be received from the proposed insured directly or indirectly via the writing agent.  As long as the request is to send the results directly to the proposed insured, a signed request from the proposed insured is not required.  If the request is to send the lab results to the proposed insured’s personal physician, then a signed authorization is required.
data.Are labs results available? Yes / No: Yes, to the proposed insured, but not to the agent or BGA.

For cases with $25,000 or greater premium:  Requirements are reviewed as they are received.

data.Other Company Exams: SBLI's preferred vendors for Exams are APPS Portamedic and ExamOne.  SBLI may approve other vendors but they will not be allowed to direct bill us. Please refer to the SBLI Underwriting Guide for additional information.
data.When does UW review the case?: Underwriters perform initial review of AU cases.  Non AU cases are reviewed upon receipt of all requirements, unless preview is needed for cause.

data.Other Company Exams: We can accept exams from other companies as long as they are provided to us. We are able to pull lab results the majoity of the time.   Subject to any change in the insured;s medical history, exams are good for 12 months (for clients under the age of 70 with standard or better risk class) or 6 months (for substandard and/or over 70. Note: If an outside paramedical part 2 is submitted, we will require a Minnesota Life non-medical part 2.)
data.When does UW review the case?: The Underwriter reviews each case within 24 hours of application entry and upon each new requirement

The field can also request that we send them if the client is unable to access via the website or if another exam company is used.

We can send the client's lab results to the client if requested to the address we have on file without a Auth to Release on file.

data.Other Company Exams: Valid for 12 months; agent to provide copy of exam, EKG if completed, and lab slip along with SML's Non-Medical Part 2. SML will access the labs.

data.Chargeback Time Period: %: Commission is charged back if premium is refunded.

Policies lapsed within first 90 days = 100%. Cincinnati will collect directly from the person/organization that was paid. The agency is not responsible for chargeback monies paid directly to the producer.

If a contract Lapses or is surrendered within twenty-four (24) months of the contract issue date, 1) all unearned first year commission will be charged back; and 2) earned commission will be charged back according to the following scale: 100% if the contract has been in force 6 months or less; 95% if in force for 7 months; 90% if in force for 8 months; 80% if in force for 9 months; 75% if in force for 10 to 12 months; 50% if in force for 13 to 18 months; 25% if in force for 19 to 24 months; and 0% if in force for 25 or more months.
data.HECV Rider: If a policy with the ECV rider lapses or is surrendered within the first five policy years, first year commissions will be charged back according to the following scale: 100% if the policy has been in-force for 2 years or less, 75% if year 3, 50% if year 4 and 25% if the policy is in year 5.

data.Chargeback Time Period: %: For lapse or Surrender: 100% within 12 months of issue date.

data.HECV Rider: With Early Cash Value Rider (Max Accumulator+ III): 100% chargeback in years 1 and 2, and 50% chargeback in years 3 and 4, includes lapses, surrenders and reduced paid up.

Certain products/riders may require that first-year commissions be paid out over several years. The payment in each subsequent year is dependent on the policy remaining in force as of the anniversary date at the beginning of that policy year. Policy lapse or surrender prior to the anniversary date will result in an elimination of future commissions payments even if the full first-year commission and SI amounts have not yet been paid out.

Certain products/riders may require that first-year commissions be paid out over several years. The payment in each subsequent year is dependent on the policy remaining in force as of the anniversary date at the beginning of that policy year. Policy lapse or surrender prior to the anniversary date will result in an elimination of future commissions payments even if the full first-year commission and SI amounts have not yet been paid out.

data.Chargeback Time Period: %: Compensation will be recaptured anytime the SVEA benefit is on the contract & a surrender takes place during the SVEA surrender benefit period, regardless if the SVEA benefit is larger than the surrender value.  The compensation recapture amount is defined as follows: 100% of comp paid in the 12 month period preceding the effective date of the surrender, plus 90% of the compensation paid in the 13th to the 24th month preceding the effective date of the surrender, plus 80% of the compensation paid in the 25th to the 36th month preceding the effective date of the surrender

Compensation will be recaptured anytime the CEVA benefit is on the contract and surrender takes place within the 1st 4 policy years: 1 - 100% of cumulative paid comp, 2 - 75% of cumulative paid comp, 3 - 50% of cumulative paid comp, 4 - 25% of cumulative paid comp

data.Benefit Amount:
Lump Sum Payments
Specified Medical Condition
Cash Surrender Value + lesser of $25,000 or 10% net amount at risk. 
Chronic Illness
Cash Surrender Value + lesser of $250,000 or 40% net amount at risk
Terminal Illness
Cash Surrender Value + lesser of $250,000 or 60% net amount at risk
Net Amount at Risk refers to approximately the existing Death Benefit plus indebtedness less accrued account value.
Maximum advance amount may be reduced by interest

Payout Benefit Option (Chronic Illness Only)
minimum advance: $500
Maximum advance and periodic payments formula:
Lesser of $1,000,000 or 50% of death benefit. Maximum advance amount divided by payout length (3-10 years) equals Annual Benefit. The annualized amount is not to exceed the lesser of the Annualized IRS Per Diem Limit or $240,000 in a given year.
data.Notes:
There is no premium cost for the Life Plus Rider. We reserve the right to charge an administrative fee up to $250 per advance (state variations may apply).

Living benefits are accessed through an advance of the policy’s death benefit, provided the insured meets eligibility requirements under the applicable rider. An advance is treated as a lien against the policy and will reduce the Death Benefit payable if not repaid. The advance will accrue interest each year. The lien may be increased if necessary to keep your policy in effect. We may charge a fee of up to $250.00 for an advance payment. The accelerated death benefit will terminate with the policy. Life insurance proceeds paid in the form of an accelerated death benefit when the insured has become chronically or terminally ill, and is otherwise eligible for benefits, are intended to receive favorable tax treatment under Section 101(g) of the Internal Revenue Code (26 U.S.C. Sec. 101(g)). There may be tax consequences in some situations in accepting an accelerated benefit payment amount. Consult your tax advisor before taking an advance. An accelerated death benefit is not to be sold as or to replace long-term care insurance, nursing home insurance, or home care insurance. An accelerated death benefit (such as the Accelerated Death Benefit Plus Rider) and long-term care insurance provide very different kinds of benefits. Receipt of Accelerated Benefit payments may adversely affect the recipient’s eligibility for Medicaid or other government benefits or entitlements. They may also be considered taxable by the Internal Revenue Service. Contact a personal tax advisor for assistance

The amount of the monthly deduction to be waived will be the Policy's monthly deduction, and the continuation guarantee account's monthly deduction, if any, multiplied by the Waiver of Monthly Deduction Benefit Percentage shown on the Rider Schedule. Note WoMD begins on the date Monthly Benefits begin under Accelerated Access Solution and will continue while Policy remains in force.

data.Notes: 18-59. Available if insured is rated Table D or better. Available at an additional cost, the Waiver of Premium Benefit Rider will waive payment of premiums if the insured becomes totally disabled.

Conversions and waiver of premium:  If the original term coverage has waiver of premium the rider will be automatically carried over to the new policy unless otherwise requested or the insured is over age 59. There are some exceptions: •	Waiver of Premium being carried over to Universal Life (other than UL Guard) or Variable Life products will become Waiver of Monthly Charges/Deductions.  •	Waiver of Premium being carried over to UL Guard, will receive the Specified Premium Amount set at zero, and only monthly charges/deductions will be waived upon disability claim.  •	If dollar amounts are specified for Waiver of Specified Premium on UL Guard policies, evidence of insurability will be needed.  •	For Universal Life or Variable Life policies, if disability benefit and Waiver of Specified charges is requested, evidence of insurability will be required.  •	If premiums on the base policy are waived under the Waiver of Premium Rider ,the only product available for conversion is the Whole Life Legacy 100. For conversions from Vantage Term, a policy owner has the additional right to convert his or her policy if:  o	The policy has a Waiver of Premium rider on the Insured in force at the end of the Level Premium Period; and  o	At that time, the Insured is totally disabled.  If converting term coverage to increase an existing policy:  •	If the existing policy to be increased has waiver of premium but the term coverage does not, the client can either have the waiver underwritten to be added to the term policy or convert to a new policy without waiver.  •	If the existing policy to be increased does not have waiver of premium but the term coverage does, the waiver cannot be carried over to the increase portion. The waiver can be removed from the term but is not required or convert to a new policy without waiver.  •	Cannot increase a policy with waiver if client is over age 59 and not on waiver claim.

Universal life policies that terminate in accordance with the grace period provision may be reinstated within five years after the expiration of the grace period.

A term policy may be reinstated, if it lapsed and was not surrendered, at any time within five years of the date of lapse. Evidence of insurability may be requested by the underwriter in order to approve the reinstatement. Payment of all premiums in arrears will be due once the policy is approved by the underwriter.

    For the scenarios with 31-day grace period, we allow an additional 25 days to send premium without out requiring Underwriting review.

This is called the Grace Period.For Permanent - We will allow 61 days from the date the policy goes into default for you to pay the Default Payment described below to bring the policy out of default. data.Notes: A Grace Period of 31 days will be allowed for the payment of any premiums, except for (i) the initial premium to put the policy In Force or (ii) any payment necessary to reinstate the policy.  If a premium is not paid at the end of the Grace Period, the policy will lapse (i.e. terminate) without value as of the due date of the unpaid premium. During the Grace Period, the insurance remains in effect unless surrendered.  If the Insured dies during the Grace Period, the Death Benefit payable will be reduced by any due and unpaid premium existing under this policy.  Any payments sent  by U.S. mail  and postmarked within the Grace Period shall be treated as received by us within the Grace Period.  Unless a rider provides otherwise, no riders will be in effect after the policy lapses.
*90 days assumes 1035 is in addition to premium. If 1035 is the only premium, then 0 days.

data.Existing Policy: Previous insurance is considered to have been replaced if new insurance is written and another policy in the Company is lapsed, surrendered or reduced (in amount) within the period extending from six months before the date of application to 12 months after, or the next premium due date, if later.  Generally, if only one policy is terminated and the annual premium is less than $100 (or if paid-up face amount is less than $5,000), it will not be considered a replacement for commission adjustment.  Note: Columbus Mutual policies transferring to Columbus Life will be treated as internal transfers rather than external transfers for commission purposes.
data.Compensation: New first-year commissions are paid only on the increased premium.   Renewal commission rate is paid in year of change for any premium not treated as first-year.   Any new term in connection with a term conversion will be considered as new business with first-year commission allowed.

Values moving from a Columbus Mutual/Columbus Life policy (loan or surrender), to our current annuity receive commission. Values moving from a Columbus Life annuity (prior to 6% front-end load) to our current annuity will receive commission.

This transaction is normally noncommissionable. The replaced policy commission will be charged back. New commission will be paid only on an increase in Minimum Premium.  If a new Universal Life policy is written and the existing Universal Life policy stays in force, premium payments on the already existing policy will be monitored. If that policy's premium payment schedule is not met, there could be a charge back of commissions against the new Universal Life policy

data.Compensation: A replacement during the first policy year falls within the commission recall period, therefore a 100% chargeback will be applied to the old policy and full compensation will be paid on the new policy. • A percentage of full compensation will be paid on the new term policy premium (net of policy fee) up to the old policy target based on duration of the replaced policy in the following chart: Duration of Replaced Policy / % of Full Compensation** 2-10* 0% 11 50% 12 60% 13 70% 14 80% 15 90% 16+ 100% *Commission Recall guidelines apply – see Commission Calculations General Information section of the Internal Replacement Guidelines **The full first year compensation calculation uses the target premium level from the replaced policy (or the target premium level of the new policy, if lower). Full first year compensation will be paid on the excess of term premium (net of policy fee) over the old policy target premium
data.Notes: No internal replacements will be allowed into any policies that do not have surrender charges or that have riders and/or benefits that waive surrender charges or enhance surrender values, such as the Exec Rider, Enhanced Surrender Value Rider, Surrender Value Enhancement Endorsement, Return of Premium Rider, etc. Excludes Lincoln MoneyGuard©. • No internal replacements of a fully underwritten product, including Lincoln LifeElements© Level Term into Lincoln TermAccel© are allowed within 12-months of contract date. • No internal replacements or conversions will be allowed between Lincoln one-year level term and Lincoln one-year level term products. • No internal replacements will be allowed between Lifestyle Select UL on either Lincoln or First Penn Pacific paper and other Lincoln products.

data.Compensation: A term policy replaced with a term policy: • In the first 18 months: Falls within the commission recall period, therefore a 100% chargeback will be applied to the old policy and full compensation will be paid on the new policy. • From 19 months through policy year 6: Will pay full first year compensation on increases in premium, if any. • In policy year 7+: Will pay full first year compensation.
data.Notes: No internal replacements will be allowed into any policies that do not have surrender charges or that have riders and/or benefits that waive surrender charges or enhance surrender values, such as the Exec Rider, Enhanced Surrender Value Rider, Surrender Value Enhancement Endorsement, Return of Premium Rider, etc. Excludes Lincoln MoneyGuard©. • No internal replacements of a fully underwritten product, including Lincoln LifeElements© Level Term into Lincoln TermAccel© are allowed within 12-months of contract date. • No internal replacements or conversions will be allowed between Lincoln one-year level term and Lincoln one-year level term products. • No internal replacements will be allowed between Lifestyle Select UL on either Lincoln or First Penn Pacific paper and other Lincoln products.

data.What to What: Single Life product to Survivorship UL/VUL
data.Compensation: Conceptually, the new survivorship policy is split into two policies for purposes of applying the internal replacementrules with respect to the rollover and the new money. If there is existing insurance on only one insured, half of the newsurvivorship policy is treated as replacing existing insurance and half of the replacement is treated as a new sale.• Full first year compensation will be paid on new money up to the increase in target premium as defined as thedifference between the target for the original insured and one-half of the new survivorship target. The excesscompensation rate will be applied to any additional new money. There are products that do not provide excesscompensation.• Reduced compensation will be paid on rollover cash value if the replaced policy for the original insured wasinforce for more than 10 years. Refer to the previous table for the percentage of full compensation based onduration.• If two single-life policies (with two different insureds) are being exchanged to a survivorship, both insuredscount as original insureds.
data.Notes: No internal replacements will be allowed into any policies that do not have surrender charges or that have riders and/or benefits that waive surrender charges or enhance surrender values, such as the Exec Rider, Enhanced Surrender Value Rider, Surrender Value Enhancement Endorsement, Return of Premium Rider, etc. Excludes Lincoln MoneyGuard©. • No internal replacements of a fully underwritten product, including Lincoln LifeElements© Level Term into Lincoln TermAccel© are allowed within 12-months of contract date. • No internal replacements or conversions will be allowed between Lincoln one-year level term and Lincoln one-year level term products. • No internal replacements will be allowed between Lifestyle Select UL on either Lincoln or First Penn Pacific paper and other Lincoln products.

data.What to What: Survivorship product to Single Life UL/IUL/VUL
data.Compensation:
• Full first year compensation will be paid on new money on the increase in target premium (determined by
comparing the new single life target to one-half of the survivorship target). The excess compensation rate will
be applied to any additional new money. There are products that do not provide excess compensation.
• Reduced compensation will be paid on rollover cash value if the survivorship policy was in-force for more than
10 years. Refer to the previous table for the percentage of full compensation based on duration.
• One exception to this rule is if you have a survivorship policy and one insured is deceased, the remaining
insured can replace the policy to a single life policy – it will qualify as a 1035 exchange. Additionally, in this
scenario the commission would be modified based upon the survivorship to single life rules (splitting the
original target in half).
data.Notes: No internal replacements will be allowed into any policies that do not have surrender charges or that have riders and/or benefits that waive surrender charges or enhance surrender values, such as the Exec Rider, Enhanced Surrender Value Rider, Surrender Value Enhancement Endorsement, Return of Premium Rider, etc. Excludes Lincoln MoneyGuard©. • No internal replacements of a fully underwritten product, including Lincoln LifeElements© Level Term into Lincoln TermAccel© are allowed within 12-months of contract date. • No internal replacements or conversions will be allowed between Lincoln one-year level term and Lincoln one-year level term products. • No internal replacements will be allowed between Lifestyle Select UL on either Lincoln or First Penn Pacific paper and other Lincoln products.

data.What to What: Single Life product to Single Life IUL/VULSurvivorship life product to Survivorship IUL/VUL
data.Compensation: New Money • Full first year compensation will be paid on the increase in target premium up to the total new money received (for products with rolling targets); the excess compensation rate will be applied to any additional new money. Note that there are products that do not provide excess compensation. o Increase in target is the difference between the original target on the replaced policy (or combined target for multiple policies) and the target of the new policy. Rollover Money • A percentage of full compensation will be paid on rollover amount based on the duration of the replaced policy in the following chart. • A replacement during the first policy year falls within the commission recall period, therefore a 100% chargeback will be applied to the old policy and full compensation will be paid on the new policy. • If a replacement occurs during policy years 2 through 10, no commission will be paid on the rollover cash value. • Full first year compensation should be calculated using the target premium level from the replaced policy that has rollover money (or the target of the new policy, if lower) and then the durational factor should be applied. • The durational factor is applied to both the target and excess portions of the calculation. Duration of Replaced Policy / % of Full Compensation 2-10 0% 11 50% 12 60% 13 70% 14 80% 15 90% 16+ 100% Note: For Duration 1, Commission Recall guidelines apply – see Commission Calculations General Information section

data.Notes: Rollover loans will be allowed if the new policy allows rollover loans. Internal Rollover loans are not commissionable. If the new plan does not allow rollover loans the loan must be paid off before the policy replacement is processed. Existing loans may be paid off using the old policy's existing cash value. The surrender charge of the replaced policy will be waived if the new policy's surrender charge is greater than or equal to the surrender charge of the replaced policy. If the surrender charge of the new policy is less, the difference between the charge on the replaced policy and the newly issued policy will be charged against the value of the replaced policy. The remaining cash value will be rolled over to the new policy.

data.Compensation: New 1st year comp rate will be paid
data.Notes: Rollover loans will be allowed if the new policy allows rollover loans. Internal Rollover loans are not commissionable. If the new plan does not allow rollover loans the loan must be paid off before the policy replacement is processed. Existing loans may be paid off using the old policy's existing cash value. The surrender charge of the replaced policy will be waived if the new policy's surrender charge is greater than or equal to the surrender charge of the replaced policy. If the surrender charge of the new policy is less, the difference between the charge on the replaced policy and the newly issued policy will be charged against the value of the replaced policy. The remaining cash value will be rolled over to the new policy.

data.Compensation:
24% of the full new business comp rate is applied to the "replaced" premium (i.e. if 1st yr comp rate is 50%, the replacement comp rate is 12% (50% x 24% = 12%).

Any new premium in excess of the "replaced" premium amount generates comp at full new business rates. See "Replacement Compensation Rules" flyer.
data.Notes: When a new policy is placed, Pru's systems generally look back 6 months and forward 6 months in search of any surrendered or lapsed policies to determine replacement comp rates or a chargeback.

data.Existing Policy: An internal exchange occurs when an SBLI life insurance policy that has been in force for less than 8 years is lapsed or surrendered within one year of the issuance of another SBLI life insurance policy for that same insured.  A life insurance policy conversion processed by contractual right is not an internal exchange.
data.Compensation: For an internal exchange to a new policy having a greater premium than the replaced policy, subtract the premium on the replaced policy from the premium on the new policy and then multiply by the applicable percentage, according to the SBLI Commission Schedule for the commission on the new policy.  For an internal exchange where the new premium is equal to or less than the premium on the replaced policy, no commission is payable.
data.Notes: Contact home office for additional information.

data.Compensation: Generally, the first year comp on the new policy will be paid at a rate equal to the renewal rate on that policy. No compensation is paid on Internal 1035 exchanges unless the contract is over 10 years old and there were no face increases in the last 3 years. If the policy is a UL product, the highest historical target prem will be reduced by the amount of the replacement prem. If the policy is Term product or a WL, no comp will be paid on any premium equal to the replacement premium.
data.Notes: EXCEPTIONS: If a term policy is in the final year of its term and is replaced by a new policy, full comp will be paid on the new policy. If a term policy, that is at least 3 years old, is replaced by a new term policy with a resulting face amount that is the greater of $250,000 higher or 20% higher, full comp will be paid on the new policy.

data.Survivorship: MassMutual currently extra contractually allows a conversion to a Survivorship Life product. Since most reinsurers will not allow conversion to survivorship, MassMutual has to retain the coverage.  There is a maximum amount that can be retained for each insured. Each insured is responsible for their half of the new Survivorship policy either through Conversion or Underwriting and one insured cannot convert coverage to cover the underwriting requirements for the other insured. This extra-contractual benefit can be changed at any time by the Company. Please reach out to  mmsdconversionquestions@massmutual.com for additional information about the process of converting to a Survivorship Life product

data.Term Policy Language: The new policy will be issued: 1. With the date of conversion as its Policy Date; 2. At the Insured's age on the date of conversion; 3. With the same rating classification as that under this Policy; 4. On any permanent life plan which we, at our sole discretion, make available for conversion and, for the amount converted, we customarily issue on the date of conversion to applicants with the Insured's rating classification; 5. With premiums based on our rates for the rating classification and plan of insurance on the date of conversion; 6. For an amount of insurance not less than our minimum for the plan provided nor greater than the face amount of this Policy on the conversion date. At least one plan of insurance will be available for conversion in an amount equal to the face amount of this Policy on the conversion date; 7. So that the time limit specified in the Incontestability and Suicide provisions of the new policy will be measured from the Issue Date of this Policy and; 8. Subject to any assignment of this Policy received at our office.

Your new policy will be on an individual, permanent life insurance plan that we make available for conversions. The Policy Date of the conversion policy will be the date of conversion. There will always be at least one plan available for conversion. Current conversion information at https://www.cinfin.com/conversion-agent
Any time before the client's anniversary on their 70th birthday or the term period.

You may select the plan and amount of insurance for the new policy. The plan must be: 1. A permanent individual life insurance plan2. A plan that is then regularly issued at the Insured's Attained Age, Premium Class of the new policy and for the amount of insurance selectedand 3. Issued by Us or by one of Our affiliated companies and made avaialble to Our policyowners for conversion purposes. You may elect from all policies made available by Us for conversion purposes, whether issued by Us or by one of Our affiliated companies, the policy which You wish to convert.

1. Conversions are available during the earlier of attained age 70 and the end of the term duration 2. During the period beginning on the Issue Date and ending on the last day of Policy Year 4, you may elect to convert this policy to any single life permanent plan of life insurance offered for sale at the time of the conversion by the Company or one of its affiliated companies. 3. Policyholders who earn a Vitality status of Gold or Platinum status on each of the three annual processing dates prior to the date of the conversion, can convert to any permanent life product during the first 10 years, rather than the 4 years if Vitality is not elected.4. Thereafter convertible to a permanent life product designated for conversions by John Hancock or one of its affiliates at the time of election. Term conversion is a no-cost feature that allows chaning from a term policy to a permanent insurance product by either NLIC or LSW. In most cases, no additional or reduced underwriting is required when a term contract is converted to permanent coverage. Conversion up to term face amount will not require evidence of insurability. Increases or decreases in face amount are not available on term policies.  Conversion credits are offered on the NLIC Term products.

Conversion credit is for conversion of the entire face amount.  Pro-rated conversion credits apply to partial term conversions.  Policyholders must request their conversion before the end of the conversion credit period in order to be eligible for for conversion credit.

SECTION 5: CONVERSION PRIVILEGE	                        	                        	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                You may convert up to the Face Amount of this Policy, without evidence of insurability, to an individual permanent life insurance policy then available from Us for Conversion from this plan, subject to the terms below.	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.1	                        CONVERSION PERIOD – You may convert this Policy on any Premium Due Date before the Conversion Available To date shown in the Schedule of Policy Benefits if the following conditions are met:	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (a) The Insured is living;	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (b) This Policy is in effect.	                        	                        	                        	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.2 CONVERSION DATE – The Conversion Date is the Policy Date of the new policy.	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.3 PARTIAL CONVERSION – If a portion of the Face Amount of this Policy is converted for a new policy as a Partial Conversion, the following terms apply:	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (a) The Face Amount of the new policy will equal the converted Face Amount on the Conversion Date. The converted Face Amount may not be less than the minimum issue limits for the new policy on the Conversion Date.	                                                                (b) The remaining Face Amount of this Policy may not be less than the minimum issue limits for this Policy.	                                                                (c) Additional benefits may be included on the new policy only with Our consent and subject to the terms and conditions of each Rider or Endorsement.	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.4 FULL CONVERSION – If the entire Face Amount of this Policy is converted for a new policy, the following terms apply:	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (a) The Face Amount of the new policy will equal the Face Amount of this Policy on the Conversion Date.	                                                                (b) If this Policy includes a chronic illness or disability Endorsement or Rider that is in effect on the Conversion Date, then the new policy may include such Endorsement or Rider. The new Endorsement or Rider will be issued on the form We are issuing on the Conversion Date. However, if the Insured is chronically ill or disabled on the Conversion Date, no chronic illness or disability Endorsement or Rider will be included in the new policy and no benefits will be paid on the new policy after the Conversion Date.	                                                                (c) Except as stated in (b) above, additional benefits may be included on the new policy only with Our consent and subject to the terms and conditions of each Rider or Endorsement.	                                                                (d) This Policy will terminate as described in Section 3.12.	                        	                        	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.5	                        HOW TO CONVERT THIS POLICY – We must receive all of the items below:	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (a) Completed application for Conversion;	                        	                        	                                                                (b) The premium for the new policy that is at least equal to the Premium Due for this Policy shown on the Schedule of Policy Benefits; and	                                                                (c) This Policy, if requesting a Full Conversion.	                        	                        	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                	                        	                        We will issue the new policy after We receive these items and approve the new policy. The Conversion is effective on the first Premium Due Date on or following Our approval of the Conversion.	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                5.6	                        TERMS OF THE NEW POLICY – The new policy will be issued subject to these terms.	                                                                	                        	                        	                        	                                                                (a) The new policy will be issued at the equivalent Premium Class then available for the plan of insurance being applied for. In the event there is more than one Premium Class for this Policy, the new policy will be issued at the equivalent Premium Class of the most recently determined Premium Class of the Insured under this Policy on the Conversion Date.	                                                                (b) The new policy will be effective as of its Policy Date, which is the same date as the Conversion Date.	                                                                (c) The amount of insurance under the new Policy may not be less than the minimum issue limits for the new policy on the Conversion Date. or more than the Face Amount of this Policy.	                                                                (d) The Insured's Issue Age on the new policy will be equal to the Policy Age on Policy Date of this Policy on the Conversion Date.	                                                                (e) The new policy will be issued on the form and at the premium rate We are using on the Conversion Date.	                                                                (f) The new policy will be subject to any Assignment and any Beneficiary designation of this Policy recorded at Our Administrative Office.	                                                                (g) If this Policy includes a chronic illness or disability Endorsement or Rider that is in effect on the Conversion Date, then the new policy may include such Endorsement or Rider. The new Endorsement or Rider will be issued on the form We are issuing on the Conversion Date. However, if the Insured is chronically ill or disabled on the Conversion Date, no chronic illness or disability Endorsement or Rider will be included in the new policy and no benefits will be paid on the new policy after the Conversion Date.	                                                                (h) Except as stated in Section 5.4 (b) above, an additional benefit may be included on the new policy only with Our consent and subject to the terms and conditions of each Rider or Endorsement.	                                                                (i) The new policy will be amended so that the time limit specified in the Incontestability and Suicide provisions will be measured from the Policy Date of this Policy. You may convert this policy to a new life insurance policy on a plan of insurance that we offer for conversion.  At least one life insurance policy form will always be made available for conversions.  We may make different plans of insurance available depending on:  the length of time that a policy has been in effect, the class of insureds to be covered, and other factors.

The new policy will be any form of life insurance policy, except term insurance, available under Our underwriting guidelines then in effect. The first premium for the new policy must be paid in advance of the policy becoming effective and is due on the new policy's Effective Date. The periods specified in the Suicide and Incontestability provisions will not begin again for the new policy, and will be measured from the Effective Date of this policy. If the new policy is not accepted, we will restore the Face Amount of Your policy to the Face Amount that existed prior to the conversion.

The new policy will be any form of life insurance policy, except term insurance, available under Our underwriting guidelines then in effect. The first premium for the new policy must be paid in advance of the policy becoming effective and is due on the new policy's Effective Date. The periods specified in the Suicide and Incontestability provisions will not begin again for the new policy, and will be measured from the Effective Date of this policy. If the new policy is not accepted, we will restore the Face Amount of Your policy to the Face Amount that existed prior to the conversion.

The Conversion Policy must be a plan of permanent insurance, such as, a flexible premium adjustable life insurance, whole life insurance, or other similar plan of life insurance available for conversion at the time of your Written Notice.  We will always have at least one such policy available.  We are not required to have more than one Conversion Policy available.

Except as we state in the next sentence, the new contract may be any life policy we or The Prudential Insurance Company of America regularly issue on its contract date for the same or equivalent rating class, amount, issue age, and sex (if applicable). It may not be: one that insures anyone in addition to the Insured or one that includes or provides for term insurance. The basic amount of the new contract may be any amount you ask for as long as it is at least $25,000 and not more than the basic amount of this contract. We will always make available at least one policy for conversion with a minimum basic amount of $25,000. If the basic amount of the new contract is less than $100,000, the new contract will not be eligible for preferred rates.

Language and definitions from sample Term policy package: Some term insurance policies are also "convertible." This means that before the end of the conversion period, you (the client) may trade the term policy for a whole life or endowment insurance policy even if you are not in good health.  Premiums for the new policy will be higher than you have been paying for the term insurance.  Subject to the terms contained herein, you (the client) have the right, upon written request, to convert all or part of the Face Amount of this policy without Evidence of lnsurability to a new policy on the life of the same Insured under this policy to a different plan of insurance.  To convert this policy, you must return it to us at our home office.  Conversion must be exercised while this policy is In Force, but no later than the End of Conversion Period shown in the Policy Specifications.  If you elect to convert all of the Face Amount of this policy, coverage under this policy will terminate.  If you elect to convert part of the Face Amount of this policy, coverage will be reduced by the amount of coverage converted, subject to the Minimum Face Amount, and premiums will be adjusted accordingly.  Any reduction in premium may not necessarily be proportionate to the reduction in Face Amount.  Except as provided herein, the new policy may be any permanent life insurance policy we make available for conversion purposes at the time of conversion.  The new policy will be issued with a Policy Date that is the same as the date we surrender this policy for its conversion ("conversion date").  This policy cannot be converted after it has terminated.  The new policy will: be for a face amount not in excess of the coverage amount provided by this policy on the conversion date; be for a face amount not less than the minimum coverage amount we then offer under the new policy; be based on the Age of the Insured on the conversion date; have premiums based on the Age of the Insured and the rates in use by us on the conversion date; be in the same risk classification as this policy or, if not available, the risk classification available that is most comparable to this one; have an Issue Date that is the same as the conversion date; if applicable, include riders providing benefits in the event of total disability if: a) the benefit is In Force under this policy on the conversion date; b) such benefit is available with new policies at the then current Age of the Insured; and c) the Insured is not totally disabled at the time of conversion.  The incontestability and suicide provisions of the new policy attributed to the coverage converted will run from the date of this original policy.  Any person to whom this policy has been transferred as collateral security must consent to conversion.  All Irrevocable Beneficiaries must consent to conversion as well.  The first premium for the new policy is due on the conversion date.
The new policy must be single life permanent coverage on the policy forms we are then issuing. The new policy must satisfy the current minimum requirements of the product to which you are converting.

The new policy may be any permanent plan of insurance available for issue as a conversion at the time of conversion for the amount converted, except for policies that provide for accelerated benefits in the event of chronic illness or long-term care or other chronic illness or long-term care benefits.

Symetra SwiftTerm: Swift Term can  convert to CAUL. Conversions may be requested prior to the end of the 10th policy anniversary or the policy anniversary following your 70th birthday, whichever is earlier.
Level Term Life: The new policy will be any form of permanent life insurance We make available to You for conversion at the time of conversion for the face amount requested.

The policy, subject to all conditions stated herein, may be converted to: 1. a non-flexible premium level premium whole life insurance plan that we make available at the date of the request, or 2. a flexible premium adjustable life insurance plan that we make available at the date of the request. At least one plan of insurance will be made available for conversion to the new policy at any time while this policy is in force.

Term Life Answers:  The new policy may be any form of permanent insurance we are issuing at that time. The following will apply: (a) The minimum required premium for the new policy must be greater than the premium for this policy at the time of conversion. (b) The face amount of the new policy may not be greater than the face amount of this policy. (c) The new policy will be based on the insured's Age at the time of conversion. (d) The premium rates for the new policy will be for a risk class and rate class at least equal to the insured's risk class and rate class under this policy. (e) If this policy has any Rider benefits, they may be included in the new policy only at our option. (f) We will not waive premiums for the new policy because of any existing disability.

Drop ticket-IGO, Appliclnt or IXN-include convenient. time for client to conduct interview. 2.The interviewer will contact client; complete Parts 1 and 2, and capture a voice or digital signature. These forms will be submitted electronically to SBLI. In addition, copies of the completed forms will be available on the fulfillment vendor website for your record. 3. Underwriting will evaluate the application with electronic data, such as prescription history, MIB, MVR and FCRA public information. The application will follow one of two paths: 1. Immediate approval that proceeds to issue. 2. Additional requirements needed as determined, such as an APS; a final assessment is determined upon the underwriter's review. If issued, an e-mail is automatically sent telling your client that their policy is available for acceptance, on our client portal www.My.SBLI.com. The policy owner can accept, sign any additional requirements, and pay entirely online.

data.Processing:
You will only need to submit completed Part I and II applications and HIPAA consent form. Electronic submissions preferred. 
	
    Symetra will run an MIB, Rx, Dx and MVR, and if the info yields a Standard rate class or better, an offer will be made.
	
    If cases submitted through this program don't receive a Standard or better offer, we will reach out and attempt to fully underwrite the case.
data.Notes:
Three potential paths to coverage


1.) Accelerated Underwriting Express: Clients ages 18-60 with face amounts up to $1 million will automatically travel through our fastest path! No exam can be submitted/pre-ordered to travel this path.

2.) Accelerated Underwriting: Light underwriting may be needed for face amounts between $1 million and $3 million. If an exam was pre-ordered, it must be reviewed.

3.) Full Underwriting: A medical examination is needed. We may also request medical records. Includes those with knockout medical conditions (see next page).

data.Most Recent COVID Guidelines:
July1, 2021-Removing most restrictions effective Tuesday, July 6, 2022. For all ages, we will continue to require a Good Health Statement. As we announced last fall for our high-net-worth foreign national market program, we have reverted to our pre-COVID underwriting capabilities (for A and B countries up to $20 million in capacity).These guidelines apply to new formal and informal cases, as well as any submitted cases that are still in underwriting review.

            We will continue to monitor updates from the U.S. Department of State, the CDC and other local, state and federal agencies, and we reserve the right to adjust these guidelines as conditions warrant.
data.Restrictions by Age: All cases up to age 79-now applying routine underwriting assessment and criteria. Our underwriting team will continue to conduct a thorough review for all cases with co-morbidities. Ages 80 and above will continue to be postponed.

data.Recovered & Exposed Ratings: Postpone for 2 weeks to 3 months, depending on if hospitalized and severity. Ongoing Long Covid-related symptoms may lead to decline.

Mutual of Omaha continues to closely monitor our new business and underwriting practices to ensure we create efficiencies and improvements when making business decisions regarding our underwriting guidelines.

Standard guidelines not published.

- Super preferred - must not have used any tobacco or nicotine based products (including patches, electronic cigarettes, cigarettes and nicotine gum) within the last 5 years.  Occasional cigar (up to 12 per year) is allowed if admitted on the application and the urine contains no nicotine. - Preferred - must not have used any tobacco or nicotine based products (including patches, electronic cigarettes, cigarettes and nicotine gum) within the last 3 years.  Occasional cigar (up to 24 per year) is allowed if admitted on the application and the urine contains no nicotine. - Standard nontobacco - must not have used tobacco or nicotine-based products (including cigarettes, e-cigarettes, vapor products, pipe, snuff, chewing tobacco, nicotine gum or patches and any other nicotine products) in any form within the last 12 months.  Occasional cigar (up to 24 per year) is allowed if admitted on the application and the urine test contains no nicotine by-products.- Tobacco - admitted or non-admitted tobacco or nicotine-based products (including cigarettes, e-cigarettes, vapor products, pipe, snuff, chewing tobacco, nicotine gum or patches and any other nicotine products) in any form within the last 12 months or any nicotine by-products in the urine test.
